拝殿 |所在地 = {{jalink|宮城県|Q47896}}{{jalink|柴田郡|Q1131788}}{{jalink|大河原町|Q1155682}}金ヶ瀬字神山45番地 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|2|18.41|N|140|41|58.55|E|region:JP-04type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=大高山神社}} |祭神 = {{jalink|ヤマトタケル|Q461258|日本武尊}}
{{jalink|用明天皇|Q349281|橘豊日尊}} |社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964|名神大}}）
旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建 = （伝）{{jalink|敏達天皇|Q316637}}元年（{{jalink|572年|Q49189}}?） |本殿 = 三間社{{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}} |別名 = 白鳥大明神、大高宮 |例祭 = {{jalink|10月15日|Q2919}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=大高山神社|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''大高山神社'''（おおたかやまじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|宮城県|Q47896}}{{jalink|柴田郡|Q1131788}}{{jalink|大河原町|Q1155682}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}。 === 祭神 === ; 主祭神 * '''{{jalink|ヤマトタケル|Q461258|日本武尊}}''' * '''{{jalink|用明天皇|Q349281|橘豊日尊}}''' ; 合祀神 * {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|迦具土命}} * {{jalink|オオヤマツミ|Q386563|大山祇命}} * {{jalink|ウカノミタマ|Q3080728|倉稲魂命}} * {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|火産霊命}} * {{jalink|菊理媛||白山菊理媛}} === 歴史 === === 創建 === 社伝によれば、大高山神社は{{jalink|敏達天皇|Q316637}}元年（{{jalink|572年|Q49189}}）に{{jalink|日本武尊}}を主祭神として創建されたという。当神社の縁起書や『安永風土記』『奥羽観蹟聞老志』（おううかんせきもんろうし）などによれば、日本武尊が蝦夷征伐のための東征の折に仮宮を立てて住んだと伝わり、その仮宮の跡地に「'''白鳥大明神'''」と称する社殿を設け、日本武尊を奉斎したという。 また伝承によれば、{{jalink|崇峻天皇|Q349238}}2年（{{jalink|588年|Q32160}}）には主祭神として橘豊日尊（用明天皇）が合祀された。これは、用明天皇が橘豊日尊と呼ばれた皇子の頃、勅命により当地へやってきたことがあり、用明天皇の皇子である{{jalink|聖徳太子|Q263972}}がその縁を持って大高山神社へ合祀したと伝わる。 === 概史 === 『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』によれば、{{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}9年（{{jalink|842年|Q24730}}）には従五位下の神階を授けられ、{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}11年（{{jalink|869年|Q23917}}）3月には従五位上に昇叙した。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}20年3月には宗源宣旨により神階最高位である正一位の位階を得た。また、大高山神社は{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}において陸奥国内百座のうちの一座であり、十五座ある{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}のうちに列する。 なお、往古は神社の北東にある金ケ瀬地区新開（旧柴田郡平村）の台ノ山に鎮座していたが、{{jalink|元禄時代}}初期に起きた火災により社殿が消失したため、金ケ瀬地区の新開126番地に遷座し、{{jalink|大正|Q329174}}3年に新開から現在の鎮座地である金ケ瀬地区の神山へと移築・再遷座した。現在も新開126番地の旧社地には鳥居と石祠が鎮座している。 File:Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 1.JPG|旧境内地 File:Small shrine of Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|旧境内地の石祠
内部に神像の頭部と神札が祀られている。 === 神階 === * {{jalink|承和 (日本)|Q1075605|承和}}9年（{{jalink|842年|Q24730}}）5月11日、無位から従五位下 （『{{jalink|続日本後紀|Q1041301}}』） * {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}11年（{{jalink|869年|Q23917}}）3月12日、従五位上 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） === 白鳥信仰と伝説 === 大高山神社には、橘豊日尊に関する伝説がある。 === 内容 === {{jalink|欽明天皇|Q329792}}の皇子である橘豊日尊は、勅命により東国巡幸の旅に出た。東国の住民は橘豊日尊を宮居を造営して心から歓待し、尊も約3年間滞在したという。その間に、当地の長者である赤坂長者の娘である玉倚姫（たまよりひめ）と恋に落ちた。 玉倚姫は性格温厚・貞淑で世にも稀な美人であったといい、尊の寵愛を受けた。ある日、玉倚姫は”白鳥が飛来して体内に入る夢”を見ると、まもなくして皇子が生まれた。尊は「陸奥国に来てから白鳥を神と崇めて祈願したことによるものだ」として大いに喜んだという。 皇子が生まれた後、尊は都への帰還命令を受けたが、別れを惜しんでそばを離れようとしない玉倚姫に「3年後には必ず迎えの使者を使わす」と約束をかわして帰っていった。 尊を想い3年が過ぎたが使者は一向に来ず、玉倚姫は病に倒れてしまう。皇子の乳母はそんな玉倚姫を見るに偲びず、皇子を抱いて河畔にやってきた。そこで「玉倚姫は今、父である橘豊日尊を想い、病で命を落とそうとしています。皇子であるあなたは神の化身であるから、どうか母君の身代わりになって父君をこの地へお戻しください」と祈り、皇子を川へと投げた。すると、皇子は不思議な事に白鳥へと変わり、深谷の鳥越の里から尊の住む大和へと飛び立った。 やがて、橘豊日尊のもとに玉倚姫の訃報が届き、玉倚姫のために立派な墓を設けて弔ったところ、白鳥が空へと飛び立ち、玉倚姫の墓の上空を日夜鳴きながら旋回したと伝わる。そのため、橘豊日尊は日本全国に白鳥を祀る『白鳥社（白鷹社）』を建てたという。大高山神社の名称も「白鷹社」が「大鷹社」に変化したものが元であるという。 === 伝説関係地 === 宮城県南部の柴田郡や刈田郡などの{{jalink|白石川|Q11580172}}沿いの地域は、ハクチョウを神の使いとして崇敬する「{{jalink|ハクチョウ|Q34384|白鳥}}信仰」と呼ばれる信仰が強い地域である。大高山神社の他にも、蔵王町宮の延喜式内名神大社である{{jalink|刈田嶺神社 (蔵王町宮)|Q11396254}}には「白鳥古碑群」という石碑群が残されている。日本武尊に関係する同様の話も残されており、刈田嶺神社の南には、日本武尊が当地に残した子供を乳母が投げ捨てた川とされる「児捨川」「児捨川橋」がある（児童虐待を連想させるいう理由により、現在は橋の名前は「白鳥橋」へ改名されている）。 File:Old stone monuments of Swan at kattamine-jinja shrine.JPG|白鳥古碑群
刈田嶺神社（蔵王町宮） === 境内 === 社殿は、拝殿・幣殿・三間社入母屋造の本殿からなる。 File:Honden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|本殿 File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 02.JPG|拝殿 * 鉄九輪塔 :: 文治年間（1185-1189年）に{{jalink|藤原秀衡|Q3090625}}の三男である{{jalink|藤原忠衡|Q11623262}}の寄進による。現在は火袋の部分のみが残され、灯籠の一部として組み込まれている。 * 南蛮鉄鳥居 :: 参道階段を登ったところにある鳥居で、室町時代末期から日本へ渡来した精鋳鉄や洋式鉄鋳などの鋼鉄を用いたものである。当社が現在の金ケ瀬地区新開126番地の旧社地に鎮座していた時に奉納されたものだという。 その他に、神輿殿、鐘楼、社務所などが境内にある。境内は桜の名所となっており、桜の時期には多くの参拝者で賑わう。 File:Iron Kurin-tower at Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|鉄九輪塔 File:Ema board of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|拝殿の絵馬
日本武尊の{{jalink|熊襲|Q1196897|クマソタケル}}討伐の様子と思われる彫刻がされている。 File:Nanban-Iron Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|南蛮鉄鳥居 File:Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 01.JPG|大高山神社鳥居 === 摂末社 === * 蚕影神社 :: 境内北側に鎮座する一間社杮葺流造の神社で、{{jalink|ワクムスビ||稚産霊命}}・{{jalink|ハニヤス|Q9285919|埴山姫命}}・{{jalink|コノハナサクヤビメ||木花開耶姫命}}を祀る。{{jalink|文治|Q1194415}}元年（{{jalink|1185年|Q19729}}）に{{jalink|常陸国|Q1055353}}{{jalink|筑波郡|Q7850092}}神郡村（{{jalink|茨城県|Q83273}}{{jalink|つくば市|Q273863}}神郡）に鎮座する蚕影神社から勧請し、{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}41年に現在地へ遷座したという。 File:Kokage-jinja shrine at Ootakayama-jinja shrine 2.JPG|蚕影神社 === 文化財 === === 重要文化財（国指定） === * {{jalink|鰐口|Q11674315}}{{Cite web|和書|title=宮城県の国・県指定文化財：鰐口|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/04Kougei/kuni/06waniguti.htm|work= |publisher=宮城県|date= |accessdate=2012-10-20}} :鎌倉末期、{{jalink|伏見天皇|Q373949}}の時代に奉納されたと伝わる。本体には長文の銘文が刻まれており、銘文中の年紀から正応6年（1293年）の製作であることが判明する。銘文冒頭には「奉献大鷹宮御宝前鰐口一口」とあり、これは銘文中に「鰐口」の語が使用された初例である『解説版 新指定重要文化財 4 工芸品I』（毎日新聞社、1981）、p.164。銘文によれば、この鰐口は、当地方の地頭であり大高山神社の社領管理を担当していた「沙弥禅益」という者とその家族の延命長寿を願うもので、大高山神社の法印であった「法橋玄應」が世話人となり神社へ奉納したものだという。 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 * {{jalink|宮城県|Q47896}}{{jalink|柴田郡|Q1131788}}{{jalink|大河原町|Q1155682}}金ヶ瀬字神山45番地 === 出典 === {{Reflist}} === 参考文献 === * 『延喜式大社 大高山神社』（大高山神社社務所） === 外部リンク === * [http://www.ohtakayama.org/ 大高山神社]（公式サイト） * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010200 大高山神社]（宮城県神社庁） * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270401.html 大高山神社]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおたかやましんしや}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:大河原町の建築物]] [[Category:大河原町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名おおたかやましんしや]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

{{jalink|Emperor Yomei|Q349281|Tachibana Toyonichi}} |Company status = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637}} 1st year ({{jalink|572 years|Q49189}}?) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{jalink|Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} |Alternative name = Shiratori Daimyojin, Otakagu Shrine |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 15th|Q2919}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Otakayama Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Otakayama Shrine'' (Otakayama Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Shibata District|Q1131788}}{{jalink|Ogawara Town|Q1155682}}. It is {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Nippon Takeru}}''' * '''{{jalink|Emperor Yomei|Q349281|Tachibana Toyonichison}}''' ; jointly enshrined deity * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kagudo no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ooyamatsumi|Q386563|Oyama Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Kurainatama-mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fireborn Spirit}} * {{jalink|Kikurihime||Hakusan Kikurihime}} === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, Otakayama Shrine was founded in the first year of {{jalink|Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637}} ({{jalink|572|Q49189}}) with {{jalink|Nippon Takeru}} as the main deity. According to the shrine's auspicious records, ``Yasunaga Fudoki'' and ``Ou Kanseki Monroshi,'' it is said that Japan Takeru built a temporary shrine and lived there during his eastern expedition to conquer Ezo, and on the site of that temporary shrine, a shrine building called ``Shiratori Daimyojin'' was built and Nihon Takeru was enshrined there. According to tradition, in the second year of {{jalink|Emperor Suushun|Q349238}} ({{jalink|588|Q32160}}) Tachibana Toyonichison (Emperor Yomei) was enshrined as the main deity. It is said that Emperor Yomei came to this area by imperial order when he was a prince called Tachibana Toyonichison, and that Emperor Yomei's prince, {{jalink|Prince Shotoku|Q263972}}, enshrined it together at Otakayama Shrine. === Outline === According to "{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301}}", in {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 9th year ({{jalink|842|Q24730}}), he was given the divine rank of Jugoinoge (Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade), and {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In March 11 ({{jalink|869|Q23917}}), he was promoted to Jugoi (Junior Fifth Rank). {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}In March 2020, he was granted the rank of Shoichii, the highest rank in the divine hierarchy, by Sogen Senji. Also, Otakayama Shrine is one of the 100 shrines in Mutsu Province in the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}, and is ranked among the 15 shrines {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}. In ancient times, it was enshrined on Mt. Daino in Shinkai, Kanegase District (formerly Hira Village, Shibata District) to the northeast of the shrine, but the shrine building was destroyed by a fire in the early {{jalink|Genroku period}}. The shrine was then relocated to 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district, and in 1992, it was relocated and relocated from Shinkai to its current enshrined location, Kamiyama in the Kanegase district. The torii gate and stone shrine still stand at the old shrine site at Shinkai 126. File:Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Former shrine grounds File:Small shrine of Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrine on the former grounds
The head of the statue and the talisman are enshrined inside. === God level === * {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 9th year ({{jalink|842|Q24730}}) May 11th, from no rank to junior fifth rank (``{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301}}'') * {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} March 12, 11th year ({{jalink|869|Q23917}}), Upper Fifth Rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') === Swan Faith and Legend === Otakayama Shrine has a legend about Tachibana Toyohichi. === Contents === Tachibana Toyonichi, the prince of {{jalink|Emperor Kinmei|Q329792}}, set out on a journey touring the eastern regions by imperial order. The residents of Togoku built a palace for Tachibana Toyonichi and warmly welcomed him, and Takashi stayed there for about three years. During that time, he fell in love with Tamayorihime, the daughter of the local ruler, Akasaka Choja. It is said that Tamakihime was a rare beauty with a gentle and chaste personality, and was loved by the noble. One day, Princess Tamaki had a dream in which a swan flew into her body and entered her body, and soon after, a prince was born. Takashi was very pleased and said, ``This is because I worshiped the swan as a god and prayed to it after coming to Mutsu Province.'' After the prince was born, Takashi was ordered to return to the capital, but he was reluctant to say goodbye to Tamakihime, who refused to leave her side, and he promised her that he would send a messenger to meet her in three years. Three years have passed since Takashi thought of Takashi, but the messenger never came and Tamakihime fell ill. The prince's wet nurse could not help but see Tamakihime in such a state, and came to the riverside carrying the prince in her arms. So, she prayed, ``Tamazuhime is thinking about her father, Tachibana Toyonichi, who is about to die of illness.As a prince, you are the embodiment of God, so please take the place of your mother and bring him back to this land,'' and threw the prince into the river. Then, the prince mysteriously turned into a swan and flew from Torigoe village in Fukaya to Yamato, where Takashi lived.Eventually, news of Princess Tamada's death reached Tachibana Toyonichison, and it is said that when he built a magnificent grave for her and buried her, a swan took off into the sky and circled over Princess Tamada's grave, chirping day and night. For this reason, Tachibana Toyonichison is said to have built ``Shiratori Shrines'' (Shirataka Shrines) to enshrine swans throughout Japan. The name of Otakayama Shrine is also said to have originated from ``Shiratakasha'' which changed to ``Otakasha''. === Legendary places === The areas along the {{jalink|Shiraishi River|Q11580172}}, such as Shibata District and Katta District in the southern part of Miyagi Prefecture, are areas where there is a strong belief in swans, called the ``{{jalink|swan|Q34384|swan}} faith,'' which reveres swans as messengers of the gods. In addition to Otakayama Shrine, a group of stone monuments known as the ``Shiratori Monument Group'' remain at {{jalink|Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine)|Q11396254}}, which is the Engishiki Naimeishin Taisha Shrine of Zao Town Shrine. A similar story related to Takeru Nihon has also survived, and to the south of Kattamine Shrine there are the ``Kosute River'' and ``Kosutegawa Bridge,'' which are said to be the rivers where Nihon Takeru threw the child he left behind by his nurse (the name of the bridge has now been changed to ``Swan Bridge'' because it is associated with child abuse). File:Old stone monuments of Swan at kattamine-jinja shrine.JPG|Swan old monument group
Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine) === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall in the Sankensha irimoya-zukuri style. File:Honden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Main shrine File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 02.JPG|Haden of worship * Iron Nine Ring Tower :: Donated by {{jalink|Fujiwara Tadahira|Q11623262}}, the third son of {{jalink|Fujiwara Hidehira|Q3090625}}, during the Bunji period (1185-1189). Currently, only the firebox remains and is incorporated as part of the lantern. * Nanban Iron Torii :: The torii gate is located at the top of the stairs on the approach to the shrine, and is made of steel such as refined cast iron or Western-style cast iron that was brought to Japan from the end of the Muromachi period. It is said to have been dedicated when the company was enshrined in its former shrine at 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district. In addition, there is a mikoshi hall, a bell tower, a shrine office, etc. within the precincts. The precincts are a famous spot for cherry blossom viewing, and are crowded with many worshipers during the cherry blossom season. File:Iron Kurin-tower at Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Iron Kurin-tower File:Ema board of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema in the worship hall
There is a carving that seems to depict the subjugation of Nippon Takeru.File:Nanban-Iron Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Nanban Iron Torii gate File:Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 01.JPG|Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine === Setsusha === * Serikage Shrine :: It is an Ikken-shrine-thatched-style shrine located on the north side of the precincts, and is dedicated to {{jalink|Wakumusubi||Wakabu Reimyo}}, {{jalink|Hannyas|Q9285919|Haniyama Hime no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Konohanasakuyabime||Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. {{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} In the first year ({{jalink|1185|Q19729}}), {{jalink|Hitachikuni|Q1055353}}{{jalink|Tsukuba District|Q7850092}} Kamigun Village It is said to have been enshrined at Kakage Shrine, which is located in ({{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Tsukuba City|Q273863}} Kami-gun), and was moved to its current location in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}41. File:Kokage-jinja shrine at Ootakayama-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Kokage Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * {{jalink|Waniguchi|Q11674315}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Miyagi Prefecture's designated cultural property: Waniguchi|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/04Kougei/kuni/06waniguti.htm|work= |publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|date= |accessdate=2012-10-20}} :It is said to have been dedicated in the late Kamakura period, during the reign of Emperor Fushimi. There is a long inscription engraved on the body, and the year in the inscription reveals that it was made in 1293. The beginning of the inscription reads, ``Dedication to Otaka Shrine Gohozen Waniguchi Bite,'' and this is the first time that the word ``Waniguchi'' is used in an inscription Explanatory Edition Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 4 Crafts I (Mainichi Shimbunsha, 1981), p. 164. According to the inscription, this crocodile mouth was dedicated to the shrine by ``Hohashi Genno'', who was the official seal of Otakayama Shrine, who took care of it and dedicated it to pray for the long life and longevity of ``Sami Zenmasu,'' a local land leader who was in charge of managing the territory of Otakayama Shrine, and his family. === Local information === ; Location * {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Shibata District|Q1131788}}{{jalink|Okawaramachi|Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * “Engishikitaisha Otakayama Shrine” (Otakayama Shrine Office) === External link === * [http://www.ohtakayama.org/ Otakayama Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010200 Otakayama Shrine] (Miyagi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270401.html Otakayama Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otakayama Shinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:大河原町の建築物]] [[Category:大河原町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名おおたかやましんしや]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

The head of the statue and the talisman are enshrined inside. === God level === * {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 9th year ({{jalink|842|Q24730}}) May 11th, from no rank to junior fifth rank (``{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301}}'') * {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} March 12, 11th year ({{jalink|869|Q23917}}), Upper Fifth Rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') === Swan Faith and Legend === Otakayama Shrine has a legend about Tachibana Toyohichi. === Contents === Tachibana Toyonichi, the prince of {{jalink|Emperor Kinmei|Q329792}}, set out on a journey touring the eastern regions by imperial order. The residents of Togoku built a palace for Tachibana Toyonichi and warmly welcomed him, and Takashi stayed there for about three years. During that time, he fell in love with Tamayorihime, the daughter of the local ruler, Akasaka Choja. It is said that Tamakihime was a rare beauty with a gentle and chaste personality, and was loved by the noble. One day, Princess Tamaki had a dream in which a swan flew into her body and entered her body, and soon after, a prince was born. Takashi was very pleased and said, ``This is because I worshiped the swan as a god and prayed to it after coming to Mutsu Province.'' After the prince was born, Takashi was ordered to return to the capital, but he was reluctant to say goodbye to Tamakihime, who refused to leave her side, and he promised her that he would send a messenger to meet her in three years. Three years have passed since Takashi thought of Takashi, but the messenger never came and Tamakihime fell ill. The prince's wet nurse could not help but see Tamakihime in such a state, and came to the riverside carrying the prince in her arms. So, she prayed, ``Tamazuhime is thinking about her father, Tachibana Toyonichi, who is about to die of illness.As a prince, you are the embodiment of God, so please take the place of your mother and bring him back to this land,'' and threw the prince into the river. Then, the prince mysteriously turned into a swan and flew from Torigoe village in Fukaya to Yamato, where Takashi lived.Eventually, news of Princess Tamada's death reached Tachibana Toyonichison, and it is said that when he built a magnificent grave for her and buried her, a swan took off into the sky and circled over Princess Tamada's grave, chirping day and night. For this reason, Tachibana Toyonichison is said to have built ``Shiratori Shrines'' (Shirataka Shrines) to enshrine swans throughout Japan. The name of Otakayama Shrine is also said to have originated from ``Shiratakasha'' which changed to ``Otakasha''. === Legendary places === The areas along the {{jalink|Shiraishi River|Q11580172}}, such as Shibata District and Katta District in the southern part of Miyagi Prefecture, are areas where there is a strong belief in swans, called the ``{{jalink|swan|Q34384|swan}} faith,'' which reveres swans as messengers of the gods. In addition to Otakayama Shrine, a group of stone monuments known as the ``Shiratori Monument Group'' remain at {{jalink|Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine)|Q11396254}}, which is the Engishiki Naimeishin Taisha Shrine of Zao Town Shrine. A similar story related to Takeru Nihon has also survived, and to the south of Kattamine Shrine there are the ``Kosute River'' and ``Kosutegawa Bridge,'' which are said to be the rivers where Nihon Takeru threw the child he left behind by his nurse (the name of the bridge has now been changed to ``Swan Bridge'' because it is associated with child abuse). File:Old stone monuments of Swan at kattamine-jinja shrine.JPG|Swan old monument group
Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine) === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall in the Sankensha irimoya-zukuri style. File:Honden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Main shrine File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 02.JPG|Haden of worship * Iron Nine Ring Tower :: Donated by {{jalink|Fujiwara Tadahira|Q11623262}}, the third son of {{jalink|Fujiwara Hidehira|Q3090625}}, during the Bunji period (1185-1189). Currently, only the firebox remains and is incorporated as part of the lantern. * Nanban Iron Torii :: The torii gate is located at the top of the stairs on the approach to the shrine, and is made of steel such as refined cast iron or Western-style cast iron that was brought to Japan from the end of the Muromachi period. It is said to have been dedicated when the company was enshrined in its former shrine at 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district. In addition, there is a mikoshi hall, a bell tower, a shrine office, etc. within the precincts. The precincts are a famous spot for cherry blossom viewing, and are crowded with many worshipers during the cherry blossom season. File:Iron Kurin-tower at Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Iron Kurin-tower File:Ema board of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema in the worship hall
There is a carving that seems to depict the subjugation of Nippon Takeru.File:Nanban-Iron Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Nanban Iron Torii gate File:Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 01.JPG|Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine === Setsusha === * Serikage Shrine :: It is an Ikken-shrine-thatched-style shrine located on the north side of the precincts, and is dedicated to {{jalink|Wakumusubi||Wakabu Reimyo}}, {{jalink|Hannyas|Q9285919|Haniyama Hime no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Konohanasakuyabime||Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. {{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} In the first year ({{jalink|1185|Q19729}}), {{jalink|Hitachikuni|Q1055353}}{{jalink|Tsukuba District|Q7850092}} Kamigun Village It is said to have been enshrined at Kakage Shrine, which is located in ({{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Tsukuba City|Q273863}} Kami-gun), and was moved to its current location in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}41. File:Kokage-jinja shrine at Ootakayama-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Kokage Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * {{jalink|Waniguchi|Q11674315}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Miyagi Prefecture's designated cultural property: Waniguchi|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/04Kougei/kuni/06waniguti.htm|work= |publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|date= |accessdate=2012-10-20}} :It is said to have been dedicated in the late Kamakura period, during the reign of Emperor Fushimi. There is a long inscription engraved on the body, and the year in the inscription reveals that it was made in 1293. The beginning of the inscription reads, ``Dedication to Otaka Shrine Gohozen Waniguchi Bite,'' and this is the first time that the word ``Waniguchi'' is used in an inscription Explanatory Edition Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 4 Crafts I (Mainichi Shimbunsha, 1981), p. 164. According to the inscription, this crocodile mouth was dedicated to the shrine by ``Hohashi Genno'', who was the official seal of Otakayama Shrine, who took care of it and dedicated it to pray for the long life and longevity of ``Sami Zenmasu,'' a local land leader who was in charge of managing the territory of Otakayama Shrine, and his family. === Local information === ; Location * {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Shibata District|Q1131788}}{{jalink|Okawaramachi|Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * “Engishikitaisha Otakayama Shrine” (Otakayama Shrine Office) === External link === * [http://www.ohtakayama.org/ Otakayama Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010200 Otakayama Shrine] (Miyagi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270401.html Otakayama Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otakayama Shinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:大河原町の建築物]] [[Category:大河原町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名おおたかやましんしや]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

link|Q11441957}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Otakayama Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Shibata District|Q1131788}}{{jalink|Okawaramachi|Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|2|18.41|N|140|41|58.55|E|region:JP-04type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otakayama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Nihon Takeru}}
{{jalink|Emperor Yomei|Q349281|Tachibana Toyonichi}} |Company status = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637}} 1st year ({{jalink|572 years|Q49189}}?) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{jalink|Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} |Alternative name = Shiratori Daimyojin, Otakagu Shrine |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 15th|Q2919}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Otakayama Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Otakayama Shrine'' (Otakayama Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Shibata District|Q1131788}}{{jalink|Ogawara Town|Q1155682}}. It is {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Nippon Takeru}}''' * '''{{jalink|Emperor Yomei|Q349281|Tachibana Toyonichison}}''' ; jointly enshrined deity * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kagudo no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ooyamatsumi|Q386563|Oyama Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Kurainatama-mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fireborn Spirit}} * {{jalink|Kikurihime||Hakusan Kikurihime}} === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, Otakayama Shrine was founded in the first year of {{jalink|Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637}} ({{jalink|572|Q49189}}) with {{jalink|Nippon Takeru}} as the main deity. According to the shrine's auspicious records, ``Yasunaga Fudoki'' and ``Ou Kanseki Monroshi,'' it is said that Japan Takeru built a temporary shrine and lived there during his eastern expedition to conquer Ezo, and on the site of that temporary shrine, a shrine building called ``Shiratori Daimyojin'' was built and Nihon Takeru was enshrined there. According to tradition, in the second year of {{jalink|Emperor Suushun|Q349238}} ({{jalink|588|Q32160}}) Tachibana Toyonichison (Emperor Yomei) was enshrined as the main deity. It is said that Emperor Yomei came to this area by imperial order when he was a prince called Tachibana Toyonichison, and that Emperor Yomei's prince, {{jalink|Prince Shotoku|Q263972}}, enshrined it together at Otakayama Shrine. === Outline === According to "{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301}}", in {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 9th year ({{jalink|842|Q24730}}), he was given the divine rank of Jugoinoge (Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade), and {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In March 11 ({{jalink|869|Q23917}}), he was promoted to Jugoi (Junior Fifth Rank). {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}In March 2020, he was granted the rank of Shoichii, the highest rank in the divine hierarchy, by Sogen Senji. Also, Otakayama Shrine is one of the 100 shrines in Mutsu Province in the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}, and is ranked among the 15 shrines {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}. In ancient times, it was enshrined on Mt. Daino in Shinkai, Kanegase District (formerly Hira Village, Shibata District) to the northeast of the shrine, but the shrine building was destroyed by a fire in the early {{jalink|Genroku period}}. The shrine was then relocated to 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district, and in 1992, it was relocated and relocated from Shinkai to its current enshrined location, Kamiyama in the Kanegase district. The torii gate and stone shrine still stand at the old shrine site at Shinkai 126. File:Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Former shrine grounds File:Small shrine of Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrine on the former grounds

The precincts are a famous spot for cherry blossom viewing, and are crowded with many worshipers during the cherry blossom season. File:Iron Kurin-tower at Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Iron Kurin-tower File:Ema board of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema in the worship hall
There is a carving that seems to depict the subjugation of Nippon Takeru.File:Nanban-Iron Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Nanban Iron Torii gate File:Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 01.JPG|Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine === Setsusha === * Serikage Shrine :: It is an Ikken-shrine-thatched-style shrine located on the north side of the precincts, and is dedicated to {{jalink|Wakumusubi||Wakabu Reimyo}}, {{jalink|Hannyas|Q9285919|Haniyama Hime no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Konohanasakuyabime||Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. {{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} In the first year ({{jalink|1185|Q19729}}), {{jalink|Hitachikuni|Q1055353}}{{jalink|Tsukuba District|Q7850092}} Kamigun Village It is said to have been enshrined at Kakage Shrine, which is located in ({{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Tsukuba City|Q273863}} Kami-gun), and was moved to its current location in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}41. File:Kokage-jinja shrine at Ootakayama-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Kokage Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * {{jalink|Waniguchi|Q11674315}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Miyagi Prefecture's designated cultural property: Waniguchi|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/04Kougei/kuni/06waniguti.htm|work= |publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|date= |accessdate=2012-10-20}} :It is said to have been dedicated in the late Kamakura period, during the reign of Emperor Fushimi. There is a long inscription engraved on the body, and the year in the inscription reveals that it was made in 1293. The beginning of the inscription reads, ``Dedication to Otaka Shrine Gohozen Waniguchi Bite,'' and this is the first time that the word ``Waniguchi'' is used in an inscription Explanatory Edition Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 4 Crafts I (Mainichi Shimbunsha, 1981), p. 164. According to the inscription, this crocodile mouth was dedicated to the shrine by ``Hohashi Genno'', who was the official seal of Otakayama Shrine, who took care of it and dedicated it to pray for the long life and longevity of ``Sami Zenmasu,'' a local land leader who was in charge of managing the territory of Otakayama Shrine, and his family. === Local information === ; Location * {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Shibata District|Q1131788}}{{jalink|Okawaramachi|Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * “Engishikitaisha Otakayama Shrine” (Otakayama Shrine Office) === External link === * [http://www.ohtakayama.org/ Otakayama Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010200 Otakayama Shrine] (Miyagi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270401.html Otakayama Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otakayama Shinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:大河原町の建築物]] [[Category:大河原町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名おおたかやましんしや]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

link|Q11441957}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Otakayama Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Shibata District|Q1131788}}{{jalink|Okawaramachi|Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|2|18.41|N|140|41|58.55|E|region:JP-04type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otakayama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Nihon Takeru}}
{{jalink|Emperor Yomei|Q349281|Tachibana Toyonichi}} |Company status = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637}} 1st year ({{jalink|572 years|Q49189}}?) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{jalink|Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} |Alternative name = Shiratori Daimyojin, Otakagu Shrine |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 15th|Q2919}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Otakayama Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Otakayama Shrine'' (Otakayama Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Shibata District|Q1131788}}{{jalink|Ogawara Town|Q1155682}}. It is {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Nippon Takeru}}''' * '''{{jalink|Emperor Yomei|Q349281|Tachibana Toyonichison}}''' ; jointly enshrined deity * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kagudo no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ooyamatsumi|Q386563|Oyama Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Kurainatama-mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fireborn Spirit}} * {{jalink|Kikurihime||Hakusan Kikurihime}} === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, Otakayama Shrine was founded in the first year of {{jalink|Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637}} ({{jalink|572|Q49189}}) with {{jalink|Nippon Takeru}} as the main deity. According to the shrine's auspicious records, ``Yasunaga Fudoki'' and ``Ou Kanseki Monroshi,'' it is said that Japan Takeru built a temporary shrine and lived there during his eastern expedition to conquer Ezo, and on the site of that temporary shrine, a shrine building called ``Shiratori Daimyojin'' was built and Nihon Takeru was enshrined there. According to tradition, in the second year of {{jalink|Emperor Suushun|Q349238}} ({{jalink|588|Q32160}}) Tachibana Toyonichison (Emperor Yomei) was enshrined as the main deity. It is said that Emperor Yomei came to this area by imperial order when he was a prince called Tachibana Toyonichison, and that Emperor Yomei's prince, {{jalink|Prince Shotoku|Q263972}}, enshrined it together at Otakayama Shrine. === Outline === According to "{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301}}", in {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 9th year ({{jalink|842|Q24730}}), he was given the divine rank of Jugoinoge (Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade), and {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In March 11 ({{jalink|869|Q23917}}), he was promoted to Jugoi (Junior Fifth Rank). {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}In March 2020, he was granted the rank of Shoichii, the highest rank in the divine hierarchy, by Sogen Senji. Also, Otakayama Shrine is one of the 100 shrines in Mutsu Province in the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}, and is ranked among the 15 shrines {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}. In ancient times, it was enshrined on Mt. Daino in Shinkai, Kanegase District (formerly Hira Village, Shibata District) to the northeast of the shrine, but the shrine building was destroyed by a fire in the early {{jalink|Genroku period}}. The shrine was then relocated to 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district, and in 1992, it was relocated and relocated from Shinkai to its current enshrined location, Kamiyama in the Kanegase district. The torii gate and stone shrine still stand at the old shrine site at Shinkai 126. File:Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Former shrine grounds File:Small shrine of Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrine on the former grounds
The head of the statue and the talisman are enshrined inside. === God level === * {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 9th year ({{jalink|842|Q24730}}) May 11th, from no rank to junior fifth rank (``{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301}}'') * {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} March 12, 11th year ({{jalink|869|Q23917}}), Upper Fifth Rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') === Swan Faith and Legend === Otakayama Shrine has a legend about Tachibana Toyohichi. === Contents === Tachibana Toyonichi, the prince of {{jalink|Emperor Kinmei|Q329792}}, set out on a journey touring the eastern regions by imperial order. The residents of Togoku built a palace for Tachibana Toyonichi and warmly welcomed him, and Takashi stayed there for about three years. During that time, he fell in love with Tamayorihime, the daughter of the local ruler, Akasaka Choja. It is said that Tamakihime was a rare beauty with a gentle and chaste personality, and was loved by the noble. One day, Princess Tamaki had a dream in which a swan flew into her body and entered her body, and soon after, a prince was born. Takashi was very pleased and said, ``This is because I worshiped the swan as a god and prayed to it after coming to Mutsu Province.'' After the prince was born, Takashi was ordered to return to the capital, but he was reluctant to say goodbye to Tamakihime, who refused to leave her side, and he promised her that he would send a messenger to meet her in three years. Three years have passed since Takashi thought of Takashi, but the messenger never came and Tamakihime fell ill. The prince's wet nurse could not help but see Tamakihime in such a state, and came to the riverside carrying the prince in her arms. So, she prayed, ``Tamazuhime is thinking about her father, Tachibana Toyonichi, who is about to die of illness.As a prince, you are the embodiment of God, so please take the place of your mother and bring him back to this land,'' and threw the prince into the river. Then, the prince mysteriously turned into a swan and flew from Torigoe village in Fukaya to Yamato, where Takashi lived.Eventually, news of Princess Tamada's death reached Tachibana Toyonichison, and it is said that when he built a magnificent grave for her and buried her, a swan took off into the sky and circled over Princess Tamada's grave, chirping day and night. For this reason, Tachibana Toyonichison is said to have built ``Shiratori Shrines'' (Shirataka Shrines) to enshrine swans throughout Japan. The name of Otakayama Shrine is also said to have originated from ``Shiratakasha'' which changed to ``Otakasha''. === Legendary places === The areas along the {{jalink|Shiraishi River|Q11580172}}, such as Shibata District and Katta District in the southern part of Miyagi Prefecture, are areas where there is a strong belief in swans, called the ``{{jalink|swan|Q34384|swan}} faith,'' which reveres swans as messengers of the gods. In addition to Otakayama Shrine, a group of stone monuments known as the ``Shiratori Monument Group'' remain at {{jalink|Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine)|Q11396254}}, which is the Engishiki Naimeishin Taisha Shrine of Zao Town Shrine. A similar story related to Takeru Nihon has also survived, and to the south of Kattamine Shrine there are the ``Kosute River'' and ``Kosutegawa Bridge,'' which are said to be the rivers where Nihon Takeru threw the child he left behind by his nurse (the name of the bridge has now been changed to ``Swan Bridge'' because it is associated with child abuse). File:Old stone monuments of Swan at kattamine-jinja shrine.JPG|Swan old monument group
Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine) === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall in the Sankensha irimoya-zukuri style. File:Honden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Main shrine File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 02.JPG|Haden of worship * Iron Nine Ring Tower :: Donated by {{jalink|Fujiwara Tadahira|Q11623262}}, the third son of {{jalink|Fujiwara Hidehira|Q3090625}}, during the Bunji period (1185-1189). Currently, only the firebox remains and is incorporated as part of the lantern. * Nanban Iron Torii :: The torii gate is located at the top of the stairs on the approach to the shrine, and is made of steel such as refined cast iron or Western-style cast iron that was brought to Japan from the end of the Muromachi period. It is said to have been dedicated when the company was enshrined in its former shrine at 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district. In addition, there is a mikoshi hall, a bell tower, a shrine office, etc. within the precincts. The precincts are a famous spot for cherry blossom viewing, and are crowded with many worshipers during the cherry blossom season. File:Iron Kurin-tower at Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Iron Kurin-tower File:Ema board of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema in the worship hall
There is a carving that seems to depict the subjugation of Nippon Takeru.File:Nanban-Iron Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Nanban Iron Torii gate File:Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 01.JPG|Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine === Setsusha === * Serikage Shrine :: It is an Ikken-shrine-thatched-style shrine located on the north side of the precincts, and is dedicated to {{jalink|Wakumusubi||Wakabu Reimyo}}, {{jalink|Hannyas|Q9285919|Haniyama Hime no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Konohanasakuyabime||Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. {{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} In the first year ({{jalink|1185|Q19729}}), {{jalink|Hitachikuni|Q1055353}}{{jalink|Tsukuba District|Q7850092}} Kamigun Village It is said to have been enshrined at Kakage Shrine, which is located in ({{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Tsukuba City|Q273863}} Kami-gun), and was moved to its current location in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}41. File:Kokage-jinja shrine at Ootakayama-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Kokage Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * {{jalink|Waniguchi|Q11674315}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Miyagi Prefecture's designated cultural property: Waniguchi|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/04Kougei/kuni/06waniguti.htm|work= |publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|date= |accessdate=2012-10-20}} :It is said to have been dedicated in the late Kamakura period, during the reign of Emperor Fushimi. There is a long inscription engraved on the body, and the year in the inscription reveals that it was made in 1293. The beginning of the inscription reads, ``Dedication to Otaka Shrine Gohozen Waniguchi Bite,'' and this is the first time that the word ``Waniguchi'' is used in an inscription Explanatory Edition Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 4 Crafts I (Mainichi Shimbunsha, 1981), p. 164. According to the inscription, this crocodile mouth was dedicated to the shrine by ``Hohashi Genno'', who was the official seal of Otakayama Shrine, who took care of it and dedicated it to pray for the long life and longevity of ``Sami Zenmasu,'' a local land leader who was in charge of managing the territory of Otakayama Shrine, and his family. === Local information === ; Location * {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Shibata District|Q1131788}}{{jalink|Okawaramachi|Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * “Engishikitaisha Otakayama Shrine” (Otakayama Shrine Office) === External link === * [http://www.ohtakayama.org/ Otakayama Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010200 Otakayama Shrine] (Miyagi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270401.html Otakayama Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otakayama Shinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:大河原町の建築物]] [[Category:大河原町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名おおたかやましんしや]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

link|Q11441957}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Otakayama Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location = {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Shibata District|Q1131788}}{{jalink|Okawaramachi|Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|2|18.41|N|140|41|58.55|E|region:JP-04type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otakayama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Nihon Takeru}}
{{jalink|Emperor Yomei|Q349281|Tachibana Toyonichi}} |Company status = {{jalink|Shikiuchisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Old {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{jalink|Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637}} 1st year ({{jalink|572 years|Q49189}}?) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{jalink|Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} |Alternative name = Shiratori Daimyojin, Otakagu Shrine |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 15th|Q2919}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Otakayama Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Otakayama Shrine'' (Otakayama Shrine) is located in {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Shibata District|Q1131788}}{{jalink|Ogawara Town|Q1155682}}. It is {{jalink|Shikiuchi}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{jalink|Yamato Takeru|Q461258|Nippon Takeru}}''' * '''{{jalink|Emperor Yomei|Q349281|Tachibana Toyonichison}}''' ; jointly enshrined deity * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kagudo no Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ooyamatsumi|Q386563|Oyama Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Ukanomitama|Q3080728|Kurainatama-mikoto}} * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Fireborn Spirit}} * {{jalink|Kikurihime||Hakusan Kikurihime}} === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, Otakayama Shrine was founded in the first year of {{jalink|Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637}} ({{jalink|572|Q49189}}) with {{jalink|Nippon Takeru}} as the main deity. According to the shrine's auspicious records, ``Yasunaga Fudoki'' and ``Ou Kanseki Monroshi,'' it is said that Japan Takeru built a temporary shrine and lived there during his eastern expedition to conquer Ezo, and on the site of that temporary shrine, a shrine building called ``Shiratori Daimyojin'' was built and Nihon Takeru was enshrined there. According to tradition, in the second year of {{jalink|Emperor Suushun|Q349238}} ({{jalink|588|Q32160}}) Tachibana Toyonichison (Emperor Yomei) was enshrined as the main deity. It is said that Emperor Yomei came to this area by imperial order when he was a prince called Tachibana Toyonichison, and that Emperor Yomei's prince, {{jalink|Prince Shotoku|Q263972}}, enshrined it together at Otakayama Shrine. === Outline === According to "{{jalink|Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301}}", in {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 9th year ({{jalink|842|Q24730}}), he was given the divine rank of Jugoinoge (Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade), and {{jalink|Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} In March 11 ({{jalink|869|Q23917}}), he was promoted to Jugoi (Junior Fifth Rank). {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}In March 2020, he was granted the rank of Shoichii, the highest rank in the divine hierarchy, by Sogen Senji. Also, Otakayama Shrine is one of the 100 shrines in Mutsu Province in the {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932}}, and is ranked among the 15 shrines {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}. In ancient times, it was enshrined on Mt. Daino in Shinkai, Kanegase District (formerly Hira Village, Shibata District) to the northeast of the shrine, but the shrine building was destroyed by a fire in the early {{jalink|Genroku period}}. The shrine was then relocated to 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district, and in 1992, it was relocated and relocated from Shinkai to its current enshrined location, Kamiyama in the Kanegase district. The torii gate and stone shrine still stand at the old shrine site at Shinkai 126. File:Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Former shrine grounds File:Small shrine of Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrine on the former grounds
The head of the statue and the talisman are enshrined inside. === God level === * {{jalink|Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|Jowa}} 9th year ({{jalink|842|Q24730}}) May 11th, from no rank to junior fifth rank (``{{jalink|Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301}}'') * {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōkan}} March 12, 11th year ({{jalink|869|Q23917}}), Upper Fifth Rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') === Swan Faith and Legend === Otakayama Shrine has a legend about Tachibana Toyohichi. === Contents === Tachibana Toyonichi, the prince of {{jalink|Emperor Kinmei|Q329792}}, set out on a journey touring the eastern regions by imperial order. The residents of Togoku built a palace for Tachibana Toyonichi and warmly welcomed him, and Takashi stayed there for about three years. During that time, he fell in love with Tamayorihime, the daughter of the local ruler, Akasaka Choja. It is said that Tamakihime was a rare beauty with a gentle and chaste personality, and was loved by the noble. One day, Princess Tamaki had a dream in which a swan flew into her body and entered her body, and soon after, a prince was born. Takashi was very pleased and said, ``This is because I worshiped the swan as a god and prayed to it after coming to Mutsu Province.'' After the prince was born, Takashi was ordered to return to the capital, but he was reluctant to say goodbye to Tamakihime, who refused to leave her side, and he promised her that he would send a messenger to meet her in three years. Three years have passed since Takashi thought of Takashi, but the messenger never came and Tamakihime fell ill. The prince's wet nurse could not help but see Tamakihime in such a state, and came to the riverside carrying the prince in her arms. So, she prayed, ``Tamazuhime is thinking about her father, Tachibana Toyonichi, who is about to die of illness.As a prince, you are the embodiment of God, so please take the place of your mother and bring him back to this land,'' and threw the prince into the river. Then, the prince mysteriously turned into a swan and flew from Torigoe village in Fukaya to Yamato, where Takashi lived.Eventually, news of Princess Tamada's death reached Tachibana Toyonichison, and it is said that when he built a magnificent grave for her and buried her, a swan took off into the sky and circled over Princess Tamada's grave, chirping day and night. For this reason, Tachibana Toyonichison is said to have built ``Shiratori Shrines'' (Shirataka Shrines) to enshrine swans throughout Japan. The name of Otakayama Shrine is also said to have originated from ``Shiratakasha'' which changed to ``Otakasha''. === Legendary places === The areas along the {{jalink|Shiraishi River|Q11580172}}, such as Shibata District and Katta District in the southern part of Miyagi Prefecture, are areas where there is a strong belief in swans, called the ``{{jalink|swan|Q34384|swan}} faith,'' which reveres swans as messengers of the gods. In addition to Otakayama Shrine, a group of stone monuments known as the ``Shiratori Monument Group'' remain at {{jalink|Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine)|Q11396254}}, which is the Engishiki Naimeishin Taisha Shrine of Zao Town Shrine. A similar story related to Takeru Nihon has also survived, and to the south of Kattamine Shrine there are the ``Kosute River'' and ``Kosutegawa Bridge,'' which are said to be the rivers where Nihon Takeru threw the child he left behind by his nurse (the name of the bridge has now been changed to ``Swan Bridge'' because it is associated with child abuse). File:Old stone monuments of Swan at kattamine-jinja shrine.JPG|Swan old monument group
Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine) === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall in the Sankensha irimoya-zukuri style. File:Honden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Main shrine File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 02.JPG|Haden of worship * Iron Nine Ring Tower :: Donated by {{jalink|Fujiwara Tadahira|Q11623262}}, the third son of {{jalink|Fujiwara Hidehira|Q3090625}}, during the Bunji period (1185-1189). Currently, only the firebox remains and is incorporated as part of the lantern. * Nanban Iron Torii :: The torii gate is located at the top of the stairs on the approach to the shrine, and is made of steel such as refined cast iron or Western-style cast iron that was brought to Japan from the end of the Muromachi period. It is said to have been dedicated when the company was enshrined in its former shrine at 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district. In addition, there is a mikoshi hall, a bell tower, a shrine office, etc. within the precincts. The precincts are a famous spot for cherry blossom viewing, and are crowded with many worshipers during the cherry blossom season. File:Iron Kurin-tower at Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Iron Kurin-tower File:Ema board of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema in the worship hall
There is a carving that seems to depict the subjugation of Nippon Takeru.File:Nanban-Iron Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Nanban Iron Torii gate File:Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 01.JPG|Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine === Setsusha === * Serikage Shrine :: It is an Ikken-shrine-thatched-style shrine located on the north side of the precincts, and is dedicated to {{jalink|Wakumusubi||Wakabu Reimyo}}, {{jalink|Hannyas|Q9285919|Haniyama Hime no Mikoto}}, and {{jalink|Konohanasakuyabime||Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. {{jalink|Bunji|Q1194415}} In the first year ({{jalink|1185|Q19729}}), {{jalink|Hitachikuni|Q1055353}}{{jalink|Tsukuba District|Q7850092}} Kamigun Village It is said to have been enshrined at Kakage Shrine, which is located in ({{jalink|Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273}}{{jalink|Tsukuba City|Q273863}} Kami-gun), and was moved to its current location in {{jalink|Showa|Q327567}}41. File:Kokage-jinja shrine at Ootakayama-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Kokage Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * {{jalink|Waniguchi|Q11674315}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Miyagi Prefecture's designated cultural property: Waniguchi|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/04Kougei/kuni/06waniguti.htm|work= |publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|date= |accessdate=2012-10-20}} :It is said to have been dedicated in the late Kamakura period, during the reign of Emperor Fushimi. There is a long inscription engraved on the body, and the year in the inscription reveals that it was made in 1293. The beginning of the inscription reads, ``Dedication to Otaka Shrine Gohozen Waniguchi Bite,'' and this is the first time that the word ``Waniguchi'' is used in an inscription Explanatory Edition Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 4 Crafts I (Mainichi Shimbunsha, 1981), p. 164. According to the inscription, this crocodile mouth was dedicated to the shrine by ``Hohashi Genno'', who was the official seal of Otakayama Shrine, who took care of it and dedicated it to pray for the long life and longevity of ``Sami Zenmasu,'' a local land leader who was in charge of managing the territory of Otakayama Shrine, and his family. === Local information === ; Location * {{jalink|Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896}}{{jalink|Shibata District|Q1131788}}{{jalink|Okawaramachi|Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * “Engishikitaisha Otakayama Shrine” (Otakayama Shrine Office) === External link === * [http://www.ohtakayama.org/ Otakayama Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010200 Otakayama Shrine] (Miyagi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270401.html Otakayama Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otakayama Shinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:大河原町の建築物]] [[Category:大河原町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名おおたかやましんしや]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

link|Q11441957}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Otakayama Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Okawaramachi|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|2|18.41|N|140|41|58.55|E|region:JP-04type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otakayama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Yomei|Q349281|WD=Q349281|lt=Tachibana Toyonichi}} |Company status = {{ill||lt=Shikiuchisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=572 years|Q49189|WD=Q49189}}?) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |Alternative name = Shiratori Daimyojin, Otakagu Shrine |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|WD=Q2919}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Otakayama Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Otakayama Shrine'' (Otakayama Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Ogawara Town|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}. It is {{ill||lt=Shikiuchi|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nippon Takeru}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Yomei|Q349281|WD=Q349281|lt=Tachibana Toyonichison}}''' ; jointly enshrined deity * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagudo no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyama Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainatama-mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Fireborn Spirit}} * {{ill|lt=Kikurihime||WD=|lt=Hakusan Kikurihime}} === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, Otakayama Shrine was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} ({{ill|lt=572|Q49189|WD=Q49189}}) with {{ill||lt=Nippon Takeru|UNKNOWN}} as the main deity. According to the shrine's auspicious records, ``Yasunaga Fudoki'' and ``Ou Kanseki Monroshi,'' it is said that Japan Takeru built a temporary shrine and lived there during his eastern expedition to conquer Ezo, and on the site of that temporary shrine, a shrine building called ``Shiratori Daimyojin'' was built and Nihon Takeru was enshrined there. According to tradition, in the second year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suushun|Q349238|WD=Q349238}} ({{ill|lt=588|Q32160|WD=Q32160}}) Tachibana Toyonichison (Emperor Yomei) was enshrined as the main deity. It is said that Emperor Yomei came to this area by imperial order when he was a prince called Tachibana Toyonichison, and that Emperor Yomei's prince, {{ill|lt=Prince Shotoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972}}, enshrined it together at Otakayama Shrine. === Outline === According to "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}", in {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730}}), he was given the divine rank of Jugoinoge (Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade), and {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} In March 11 ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|WD=Q23917}}), he was promoted to Jugoi (Junior Fifth Rank). {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}In March 2020, he was granted the rank of Shoichii, the highest rank in the divine hierarchy, by Sogen Senji. Also, Otakayama Shrine is one of the 100 shrines in Mutsu Province in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, and is ranked among the 15 shrines {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}. In ancient times, it was enshrined on Mt. Daino in Shinkai, Kanegase District (formerly Hira Village, Shibata District) to the northeast of the shrine, but the shrine building was destroyed by a fire in the early {{ill||lt=Genroku period|UNKNOWN}}. The shrine was then relocated to 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district, and in 1992, it was relocated and relocated from Shinkai to its current enshrined location, Kamiyama in the Kanegase district. The torii gate and stone shrine still stand at the old shrine site at Shinkai 126. File:Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Former shrine grounds File:Small shrine of Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrine on the former grounds
The head of the statue and the talisman are enshrined inside. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730}}) May 11th, from no rank to junior fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'') * {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} March 12, 11th year ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|WD=Q23917}}), Upper Fifth Rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') === Swan Faith and Legend === Otakayama Shrine has a legend about Tachibana Toyohichi. === Contents === Tachibana Toyonichi, the prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792}}, set out on a journey touring the eastern regions by imperial order. The residents of Togoku built a palace for Tachibana Toyonichi and warmly welcomed him, and Takashi stayed there for about three years. During that time, he fell in love with Tamayorihime, the daughter of the local ruler, Akasaka Choja. It is said that Tamakihime was a rare beauty with a gentle and chaste personality, and was loved by the noble. One day, Princess Tamaki had a dream in which a swan flew into her body and entered her body, and soon after, a prince was born. Takashi was very pleased and said, ``This is because I worshiped the swan as a god and prayed to it after coming to Mutsu Province.'' After the prince was born, Takashi was ordered to return to the capital, but he was reluctant to say goodbye to Tamakihime, who refused to leave her side, and he promised her that he would send a messenger to meet her in three years. Three years have passed since Takashi thought of Takashi, but the messenger never came and Tamakihime fell ill. The prince's wet nurse could not help but see Tamakihime in such a state, and came to the riverside carrying the prince in her arms. So, she prayed, ``Tamazuhime is thinking about her father, Tachibana Toyonichi, who is about to die of illness.As a prince, you are the embodiment of God, so please take the place of your mother and bring him back to this land,'' and threw the prince into the river. Then, the prince mysteriously turned into a swan and flew from Torigoe village in Fukaya to Yamato, where Takashi lived.Eventually, news of Princess Tamada's death reached Tachibana Toyonichison, and it is said that when he built a magnificent grave for her and buried her, a swan took off into the sky and circled over Princess Tamada's grave, chirping day and night. For this reason, Tachibana Toyonichison is said to have built ``Shiratori Shrines'' (Shirataka Shrines) to enshrine swans throughout Japan. The name of Otakayama Shrine is also said to have originated from ``Shiratakasha'' which changed to ``Otakasha''. === Legendary places === The areas along the {{ill|lt=Shiraishi River|Q11580172|WD=Q11580172}}, such as Shibata District and Katta District in the southern part of Miyagi Prefecture, are areas where there is a strong belief in swans, called the ``{{ill|lt=swan|Q34384|WD=Q34384|lt=swan}} faith,'' which reveres swans as messengers of the gods. In addition to Otakayama Shrine, a group of stone monuments known as the ``Shiratori Monument Group'' remain at {{ill|lt=Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine)|Q11396254|WD=Q11396254}}, which is the Engishiki Naimeishin Taisha Shrine of Zao Town Shrine. A similar story related to Takeru Nihon has also survived, and to the south of Kattamine Shrine there are the ``Kosute River'' and ``Kosutegawa Bridge,'' which are said to be the rivers where Nihon Takeru threw the child he left behind by his nurse (the name of the bridge has now been changed to ``Swan Bridge'' because it is associated with child abuse). File:Old stone monuments of Swan at kattamine-jinja shrine.JPG|Swan old monument group
Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine) === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall in the Sankensha irimoya-zukuri style. File:Honden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Main shrine File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 02.JPG|Haden of worship * Iron Nine Ring Tower :: Donated by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Tadahira|Q11623262|WD=Q11623262}}, the third son of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Hidehira|Q3090625|WD=Q3090625}}, during the Bunji period (1185-1189). Currently, only the firebox remains and is incorporated as part of the lantern. * Nanban Iron Torii :: The torii gate is located at the top of the stairs on the approach to the shrine, and is made of steel such as refined cast iron or Western-style cast iron that was brought to Japan from the end of the Muromachi period. It is said to have been dedicated when the company was enshrined in its former shrine at 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district. In addition, there is a mikoshi hall, a bell tower, a shrine office, etc. within the precincts. The precincts are a famous spot for cherry blossom viewing, and are crowded with many worshipers during the cherry blossom season. File:Iron Kurin-tower at Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Iron Kurin-tower File:Ema board of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema in the worship hall
There is a carving that seems to depict the subjugation of Nippon Takeru.File:Nanban-Iron Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Nanban Iron Torii gate File:Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 01.JPG|Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine === Setsusha === * Serikage Shrine :: It is an Ikken-shrine-thatched-style shrine located on the north side of the precincts, and is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Wakumusubi||WD=|lt=Wakabu Reimyo}}, {{ill|lt=Hannyas|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyama Hime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyabime||WD=|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} In the first year ({{ill|lt=1185|Q19729|WD=Q19729}}), {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}{{ill|lt=Tsukuba District|Q7850092|WD=Q7850092}} Kamigun Village It is said to have been enshrined at Kakage Shrine, which is located in ({{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Tsukuba City|Q273863|WD=Q273863}} Kami-gun), and was moved to its current location in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}41. File:Kokage-jinja shrine at Ootakayama-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Kokage Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * {{ill|lt=Waniguchi|Q11674315|WD=Q11674315}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Miyagi Prefecture's designated cultural property: Waniguchi|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/04Kougei/kuni/06waniguti.htm|work= |publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|date= |accessdate=2012-10-20}} :It is said to have been dedicated in the late Kamakura period, during the reign of Emperor Fushimi. There is a long inscription engraved on the body, and the year in the inscription reveals that it was made in 1293. The beginning of the inscription reads, ``Dedication to Otaka Shrine Gohozen Waniguchi Bite,'' and this is the first time that the word ``Waniguchi'' is used in an inscription Explanatory Edition Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 4 Crafts I (Mainichi Shimbunsha, 1981), p. 164. According to the inscription, this crocodile mouth was dedicated to the shrine by ``Hohashi Genno'', who was the official seal of Otakayama Shrine, who took care of it and dedicated it to pray for the long life and longevity of ``Sami Zenmasu,'' a local land leader who was in charge of managing the territory of Otakayama Shrine, and his family. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Okawaramachi|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * “Engishikitaisha Otakayama Shrine” (Otakayama Shrine Office) === External link === * [http://www.ohtakayama.org/ Otakayama Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010200 Otakayama Shrine] (Miyagi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270401.html Otakayama Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otakayama Shinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:大河原町の建築物]] [[Category:大河原町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名おおたかやましんしや]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]
