link|Q11441957}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Otakayama Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Okawaramachi|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|2|18.41|N|140|41|58.55|E|region:JP-04type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otakayama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Yomei|Q349281|WD=Q349281|lt=Tachibana Toyonichi}} |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=572 years|Q49189|WD=Q49189}}?) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |Alternative name = Shiratori Daimyojin, Otakagu Shrine |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|WD=Q2919}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Otakayama Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Otakayama Shrine'' (Otakayama Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Ogawara Town|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nippon Takeru}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Yomei|Q349281|WD=Q349281|lt=Tachibana Toyonichison}}''' ; jointly enshrined deity * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagudo no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyama Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainatama-mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Fireborn Spirit}} * {{ill|lt=Kikurihime|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Kikurihime}} === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, Otakayama Shrine was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} ({{ill|lt=572|Q49189|WD=Q49189}}) with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nippon Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} as the main deity. According to the shrine's auspicious records, ``Yasunaga Fudoki'' and ``Ou Kanseki Monroshi,'' it is said that Japan Takeru built a temporary shrine and lived there during his eastern expedition to conquer Ezo, and on the site of that temporary shrine, a shrine building called ``Shiratori Daimyojin'' was built and Nihon Takeru was enshrined there. According to tradition, in the second year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suushun|Q349238|WD=Q349238}} ({{ill|lt=588|Q32160|WD=Q32160}}) Tachibana Toyonichison (Emperor Yomei) was enshrined as the main deity. It is said that Emperor Yomei came to this area by imperial order when he was a prince called Tachibana Toyonichison, and that Emperor Yomei's prince, {{ill|lt=Prince Shotoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972}}, enshrined it together at Otakayama Shrine. === Outline === According to "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}", in {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730}}), he was given the divine rank of Jugoinoge (Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade), and {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} In March 11 ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|WD=Q23917}}), he was promoted to Jugoi (Junior Fifth Rank). {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}In March 2020, he was granted the rank of Shoichii, the highest rank in the divine hierarchy, by Sogen Senji. Also, Otakayama Shrine is one of the 100 shrines in Mutsu Province in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, and is ranked among the 15 shrines {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}. In ancient times, it was enshrined on Mt. Daino in Shinkai, Kanegase District (formerly Hira Village, Shibata District) to the northeast of the shrine, but the shrine building was destroyed by a fire in the early {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Genroku period|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine was then relocated to 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district, and in 1992, it was relocated and relocated from Shinkai to its current enshrined location, Kamiyama in the Kanegase district. The torii gate and stone shrine still stand at the old shrine site at Shinkai 126. File:Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Former shrine grounds File:Small shrine of Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrine on the former grounds
The head of the statue and the talisman are enshrined inside. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730}}) May 11th, from no rank to junior fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'') * {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} March 12, 11th year ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|WD=Q23917}}), Upper Fifth Rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') === Swan Faith and Legend === Otakayama Shrine has a legend about Tachibana Toyohichi. === Contents === Tachibana Toyonichi, the prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792}}, set out on a journey touring the eastern regions by imperial order. The residents of Togoku built a palace for Tachibana Toyonichi and warmly welcomed him, and Takashi stayed there for about three years. During that time, he fell in love with Tamayorihime, the daughter of the local ruler, Akasaka Choja. It is said that Tamakihime was a rare beauty with a gentle and chaste personality, and was loved by the noble. One day, Princess Tamaki had a dream in which a swan flew into her body and entered her body, and soon after, a prince was born. Takashi was very pleased and said, ``This is because I worshiped the swan as a god and prayed to it after coming to Mutsu Province.'' After the prince was born, Takashi was ordered to return to the capital, but he was reluctant to say goodbye to Tamakihime, who refused to leave her side, and he promised her that he would send a messenger to meet her in three years. Three years have passed since Takashi thought of Takashi, but the messenger never came and Tamakihime fell ill. The prince's wet nurse could not help but see Tamakihime in such a state, and came to the riverside carrying the prince in her arms. So, she prayed, ``Tamazuhime is thinking about her father, Tachibana Toyonichi, who is about to die of illness.As a prince, you are the embodiment of God, so please take the place of your mother and bring him back to this land,'' and threw the prince into the river. Then, the prince mysteriously turned into a swan and flew from Torigoe village in Fukaya to Yamato, where Takashi lived.Eventually, news of Princess Tamada's death reached Tachibana Toyonichison, and it is said that when he built a magnificent grave for her and buried her, a swan took off into the sky and circled over Princess Tamada's grave, chirping day and night. For this reason, Tachibana Toyonichison is said to have built ``Shiratori Shrines'' (Shirataka Shrines) to enshrine swans throughout Japan. The name of Otakayama Shrine is also said to have originated from ``Shiratakasha'' which changed to ``Otakasha''. === Legendary places === The areas along the {{ill|lt=Shiraishi River|Q11580172|WD=Q11580172}}, such as Shibata District and Katta District in the southern part of Miyagi Prefecture, are areas where there is a strong belief in swans, called the ``{{ill|lt=swan|Q34384|WD=Q34384|lt=swan}} faith,'' which reveres swans as messengers of the gods. In addition to Otakayama Shrine, a group of stone monuments known as the ``Shiratori Monument Group'' remain at {{ill|lt=Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine)|Q11396254|WD=Q11396254}}, which is the Engishiki Naimeishin Taisha Shrine of Zao Town Shrine. A similar story related to Takeru Nihon has also survived, and to the south of Kattamine Shrine there are the ``Kosute River'' and ``Kosutegawa Bridge,'' which are said to be the rivers where Nihon Takeru threw the child he left behind by his nurse (the name of the bridge has now been changed to ``Swan Bridge'' because it is associated with child abuse). File:Old stone monuments of Swan at kattamine-jinja shrine.JPG|Swan old monument group
Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine) === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall in the Sankensha irimoya-zukuri style. File:Honden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Main shrine File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 02.JPG|Haden of worship * Iron Nine Ring Tower :: Donated by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Tadahira|Q11623262|WD=Q11623262}}, the third son of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Hidehira|Q3090625|WD=Q3090625}}, during the Bunji period (1185-1189). Currently, only the firebox remains and is incorporated as part of the lantern. * Nanban Iron Torii :: The torii gate is located at the top of the stairs on the approach to the shrine, and is made of steel such as refined cast iron or Western-style cast iron that was brought to Japan from the end of the Muromachi period. It is said to have been dedicated when the company was enshrined in its former shrine at 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district. In addition, there is a mikoshi hall, a bell tower, a shrine office, etc. within the precincts. The precincts are a famous spot for cherry blossom viewing, and are crowded with many worshipers during the cherry blossom season. File:Iron Kurin-tower at Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Iron Kurin-tower File:Ema board of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema in the worship hall
There is a carving that seems to depict the subjugation of Nippon Takeru.File:Nanban-Iron Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Nanban Iron Torii gate File:Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 01.JPG|Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine === Setsusha === * Serikage Shrine :: It is an Ikken-shrine-thatched-style shrine located on the north side of the precincts, and is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakabu Reimyo}}, {{ill|lt=Hannyas|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyama Hime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyabime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} In the first year ({{ill|lt=1185|Q19729|WD=Q19729}}), {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}{{ill|lt=Tsukuba District|Q7850092|WD=Q7850092}} Kamigun Village It is said to have been enshrined at Kakage Shrine, which is located in ({{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Tsukuba City|Q273863|WD=Q273863}} Kami-gun), and was moved to its current location in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}41. File:Kokage-jinja shrine at Ootakayama-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Kokage Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * {{ill|lt=Waniguchi|Q11674315|WD=Q11674315}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Miyagi Prefecture's designated cultural property: Waniguchi|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/04Kougei/kuni/06waniguti.htm|work= |publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|date= |accessdate=2012-10-20}} :It is said to have been dedicated in the late Kamakura period, during the reign of Emperor Fushimi. There is a long inscription engraved on the body, and the year in the inscription reveals that it was made in 1293. The beginning of the inscription reads, ``Dedication to Otaka Shrine Gohozen Waniguchi Bite,'' and this is the first time that the word ``Waniguchi'' is used in an inscription Explanatory Edition Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 4 Crafts I (Mainichi Shimbunsha, 1981), p. 164. According to the inscription, this crocodile mouth was dedicated to the shrine by ``Hohashi Genno'', who was the official seal of Otakayama Shrine, who took care of it and dedicated it to pray for the long life and longevity of ``Sami Zenmasu,'' a local land leader who was in charge of managing the territory of Otakayama Shrine, and his family. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Okawaramachi|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * “Engishikitaisha Otakayama Shrine” (Otakayama Shrine Office) === External link === * [http://www.ohtakayama.org/ Otakayama Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010200 Otakayama Shrine] (Miyagi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270401.html Otakayama Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otakayama Shinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:大河原町の建築物]] [[Category:大河原町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名おおたかやましんしや]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Okawaramachi|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|2|18.41|N|140|41|58.55|E|region:JP-04type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otakayama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Yomei|Q349281|WD=Q349281|lt=Tachibana Toyonichi}} |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=572 years|Q49189|WD=Q49189}}?) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |Alternative name = Shiratori Daimyojin, Otakagu Shrine |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|WD=Q2919}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Otakayama Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Otakayama Shrine'' (Otakayama Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Ogawara Town|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nippon Takeru}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Yomei|Q349281|WD=Q349281|lt=Tachibana Toyonichison}}''' ; jointly enshrined deity * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagudo no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyama Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainatama-mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Fireborn Spirit}} * {{ill|lt=Kikurihime|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Kikurihime}} === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, Otakayama Shrine was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} ({{ill|lt=572|Q49189|WD=Q49189}}) with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nippon Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} as the main deity. According to the shrine's auspicious records, ``Yasunaga Fudoki'' and ``Ou Kanseki Monroshi,'' it is said that Japan Takeru built a temporary shrine and lived there during his eastern expedition to conquer Ezo, and on the site of that temporary shrine, a shrine building called ``Shiratori Daimyojin'' was built and Nihon Takeru was enshrined there. According to tradition, in the second year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suushun|Q349238|WD=Q349238}} ({{ill|lt=588|Q32160|WD=Q32160}}) Tachibana Toyonichison (Emperor Yomei) was enshrined as the main deity. It is said that Emperor Yomei came to this area by imperial order when he was a prince called Tachibana Toyonichison, and that Emperor Yomei's prince, {{ill|lt=Prince Shotoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972}}, enshrined it together at Otakayama Shrine. === Outline === According to "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}", in {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730}}), he was given the divine rank of Jugoinoge (Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade), and {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} In March 11 ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|WD=Q23917}}), he was promoted to Jugoi (Junior Fifth Rank). {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}In March 2020, he was granted the rank of Shoichii, the highest rank in the divine hierarchy, by Sogen Senji. Also, Otakayama Shrine is one of the 100 shrines in Mutsu Province in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, and is ranked among the 15 shrines {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}. In ancient times, it was enshrined on Mt. Daino in Shinkai, Kanegase District (formerly Hira Village, Shibata District) to the northeast of the shrine, but the shrine building was destroyed by a fire in the early {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Genroku period|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine was then relocated to 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district, and in 1992, it was relocated and relocated from Shinkai to its current enshrined location, Kamiyama in the Kanegase district. The torii gate and stone shrine still stand at the old shrine site at Shinkai 126. File:Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Former shrine grounds File:Small shrine of Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrine on the former grounds
The head of the statue and the talisman are enshrined inside. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730}}) May 11th, from no rank to junior fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'') * {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} March 12, 11th year ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|WD=Q23917}}), Upper Fifth Rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') === Swan Faith and Legend === Otakayama Shrine has a legend about Tachibana Toyohichi. === Contents === Tachibana Toyonichi, the prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792}}, set out on a journey touring the eastern regions by imperial order. The residents of Togoku built a palace for Tachibana Toyonichi and warmly welcomed him, and Takashi stayed there for about three years. During that time, he fell in love with Tamayorihime, the daughter of the local ruler, Akasaka Choja. It is said that Tamakihime was a rare beauty with a gentle and chaste personality, and was loved by the noble. One day, Princess Tamaki had a dream in which a swan flew into her body and entered her body, and soon after, a prince was born. Takashi was very pleased and said, ``This is because I worshiped the swan as a god and prayed to it after coming to Mutsu Province.'' After the prince was born, Takashi was ordered to return to the capital, but he was reluctant to say goodbye to Tamakihime, who refused to leave her side, and he promised her that he would send a messenger to meet her in three years. Three years have passed since Takashi thought of Takashi, but the messenger never came and Tamakihime fell ill. The prince's wet nurse could not help but see Tamakihime in such a state, and came to the riverside carrying the prince in her arms. So, she prayed, ``Tamazuhime is thinking about her father, Tachibana Toyonichi, who is about to die of illness.As a prince, you are the embodiment of God, so please take the place of your mother and bring him back to this land,'' and threw the prince into the river. Then, the prince mysteriously turned into a swan and flew from Torigoe village in Fukaya to Yamato, where Takashi lived.Eventually, news of Princess Tamada's death reached Tachibana Toyonichison, and it is said that when he built a magnificent grave for her and buried her, a swan took off into the sky and circled over Princess Tamada's grave, chirping day and night. For this reason, Tachibana Toyonichison is said to have built ``Shiratori Shrines'' (Shirataka Shrines) to enshrine swans throughout Japan. The name of Otakayama Shrine is also said to have originated from ``Shiratakasha'' which changed to ``Otakasha''. === Legendary places === The areas along the {{ill|lt=Shiraishi River|Q11580172|WD=Q11580172}}, such as Shibata District and Katta District in the southern part of Miyagi Prefecture, are areas where there is a strong belief in swans, called the ``{{ill|lt=swan|Q34384|WD=Q34384|lt=swan}} faith,'' which reveres swans as messengers of the gods. In addition to Otakayama Shrine, a group of stone monuments known as the ``Shiratori Monument Group'' remain at {{ill|lt=Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine)|Q11396254|WD=Q11396254}}, which is the Engishiki Naimeishin Taisha Shrine of Zao Town Shrine. A similar story related to Takeru Nihon has also survived, and to the south of Kattamine Shrine there are the ``Kosute River'' and ``Kosutegawa Bridge,'' which are said to be the rivers where Nihon Takeru threw the child he left behind by his nurse (the name of the bridge has now been changed to ``Swan Bridge'' because it is associated with child abuse). File:Old stone monuments of Swan at kattamine-jinja shrine.JPG|Swan old monument group
Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine) === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall in the Sankensha irimoya-zukuri style. File:Honden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Main shrine File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 02.JPG|Haden of worship * Iron Nine Ring Tower :: Donated by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Tadahira|Q11623262|WD=Q11623262}}, the third son of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Hidehira|Q3090625|WD=Q3090625}}, during the Bunji period (1185-1189). Currently, only the firebox remains and is incorporated as part of the lantern. * Nanban Iron Torii :: The torii gate is located at the top of the stairs on the approach to the shrine, and is made of steel such as refined cast iron or Western-style cast iron that was brought to Japan from the end of the Muromachi period. It is said to have been dedicated when the company was enshrined in its former shrine at 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district. In addition, there is a mikoshi hall, a bell tower, a shrine office, etc. within the precincts. The precincts are a famous spot for cherry blossom viewing, and are crowded with many worshipers during the cherry blossom season. File:Iron Kurin-tower at Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Iron Kurin-tower File:Ema board of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema in the worship hall
There is a carving that seems to depict the subjugation of Nippon Takeru.File:Nanban-Iron Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Nanban Iron Torii gate File:Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 01.JPG|Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine === Setsusha === * Serikage Shrine :: It is an Ikken-shrine-thatched-style shrine located on the north side of the precincts, and is dedicated to {{ill|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakabu Reimyo}}, {{ill|lt=Hannyas|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyama Hime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyabime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} In the first year ({{ill|lt=1185|Q19729|WD=Q19729}}), {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}{{ill|lt=Tsukuba District|Q7850092|WD=Q7850092}} Kamigun Village It is said to have been enshrined at Kakage Shrine, which is located in ({{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Tsukuba City|Q273863|WD=Q273863}} Kami-gun), and was moved to its current location in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}41. File:Kokage-jinja shrine at Ootakayama-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Kokage Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * {{ill|lt=Waniguchi|Q11674315|WD=Q11674315}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Miyagi Prefecture's designated cultural property: Waniguchi|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/04Kougei/kuni/06waniguti.htm|work= |publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|date= |accessdate=2012-10-20}} :It is said to have been dedicated in the late Kamakura period, during the reign of Emperor Fushimi. There is a long inscription engraved on the body, and the year in the inscription reveals that it was made in 1293. The beginning of the inscription reads, ``Dedication to Otaka Shrine Gohozen Waniguchi Bite,'' and this is the first time that the word ``Waniguchi'' is used in an inscription Explanatory Edition Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 4 Crafts I (Mainichi Shimbunsha, 1981), p. 164. According to the inscription, this crocodile mouth was dedicated to the shrine by ``Hohashi Genno'', who was the official seal of Otakayama Shrine, who took care of it and dedicated it to pray for the long life and longevity of ``Sami Zenmasu,'' a local land leader who was in charge of managing the territory of Otakayama Shrine, and his family. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Okawaramachi|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * “Engishikitaisha Otakayama Shrine” (Otakayama Shrine Office) === External link === * [http://www.ohtakayama.org/ Otakayama Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010200 Otakayama Shrine] (Miyagi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270401.html Otakayama Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otakayama Shinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:大河原町の建築物]] [[Category:大河原町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名おおたかやましんしや]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

link|Q11441957}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Otakayama Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Okawaramachi|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|2|18.41|N|140|41|58.55|E|region:JP-04type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otakayama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Yomei|Q349281|WD=Q349281|lt=Tachibana Toyonichi}} |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=572 years|Q49189|WD=Q49189}}?) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |Alternative name = Shiratori Daimyojin, Otakagu Shrine |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|WD=Q2919}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Otakayama Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Otakayama Shrine'' (Otakayama Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Ogawara Town|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nippon Takeru}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Yomei|Q349281|WD=Q349281|lt=Tachibana Toyonichison}}''' ; jointly enshrined deity * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagudo no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyama Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainatama-mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Fireborn Spirit}} * {{ill|lt=Kikurihime|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Kikurihime}} === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, Otakayama Shrine was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} ({{ill|lt=572|Q49189|WD=Q49189}}) with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nippon Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} as the main deity. According to the shrine's auspicious records, ``Yasunaga Fudoki'' and ``Ou Kanseki Monroshi,'' it is said that Japan Takeru built a temporary shrine and lived there during his eastern expedition to conquer Ezo, and on the site of that temporary shrine, a shrine building called ``Shiratori Daimyojin'' was built and Nihon Takeru was enshrined there. According to tradition, in the second year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suushun|Q349238|WD=Q349238}} ({{ill|lt=588|Q32160|WD=Q32160}}) Tachibana Toyonichison (Emperor Yomei) was enshrined as the main deity. It is said that Emperor Yomei came to this area by imperial order when he was a prince called Tachibana Toyonichison, and that Emperor Yomei's prince, {{ill|lt=Prince Shotoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972}}, enshrined it together at Otakayama Shrine. === Outline === According to "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}", in {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730}}), he was given the divine rank of Jugoinoge (Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade), and {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} In March 11 ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|WD=Q23917}}), he was promoted to Jugoi (Junior Fifth Rank). {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}In March 2020, he was granted the rank of Shoichii, the highest rank in the divine hierarchy, by Sogen Senji. Also, Otakayama Shrine is one of the 100 shrines in Mutsu Province in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, and is ranked among the 15 shrines {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}. In ancient times, it was enshrined on Mt. Daino in Shinkai, Kanegase District (formerly Hira Village, Shibata District) to the northeast of the shrine, but the shrine building was destroyed by a fire in the early {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Genroku period|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine was then relocated to 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district, and in 1992, it was relocated and relocated from Shinkai to its current enshrined location, Kamiyama in the Kanegase district. The torii gate and stone shrine still stand at the old shrine site at Shinkai 126. File:Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Former shrine grounds File:Small shrine of Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrine on the former grounds
The head of the statue and the talisman are enshrined inside. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730}}) May 11th, from no rank to junior fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'') * {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} March 12, 11th year ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|WD=Q23917}}), Upper Fifth Rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') === Swan Faith and Legend === Otakayama Shrine has a legend about Tachibana Toyohichi. === Contents === Tachibana Toyonichi, the prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792}}, set out on a journey touring the eastern regions by imperial order. The residents of Togoku built a palace for Tachibana Toyonichi and warmly welcomed him, and Takashi stayed there for about three years. During that time, he fell in love with Tamayorihime, the daughter of the local ruler, Akasaka Choja. It is said that Tamakihime was a rare beauty with a gentle and chaste personality, and was loved by the noble. One day, Princess Tamaki had a dream in which a swan flew into her body and entered her body, and soon after, a prince was born. Takashi was very pleased and said, ``This is because I worshiped the swan as a god and prayed to it after coming to Mutsu Province.'' After the prince was born, Takashi was ordered to return to the capital, but he was reluctant to say goodbye to Tamakihime, who refused to leave her side, and he promised her that he would send a messenger to meet her in three years. Three years have passed since Takashi thought of Takashi, but the messenger never came and Tamakihime fell ill. The prince's wet nurse could not help but see Tamakihime in such a state, and came to the riverside carrying the prince in her arms. So, she prayed, ``Tamazuhime is thinking about her father, Tachibana Toyonichi, who is about to die of illness.As a prince, you are the embodiment of God, so please take the place of your mother and bring him back to this land,'' and threw the prince into the river. Then, the prince mysteriously turned into a swan and flew from Torigoe village in Fukaya to Yamato, where Takashi lived.Eventually, news of Princess Tamada's death reached Tachibana Toyonichison, and it is said that when he built a magnificent grave for her and buried her, a swan took off into the sky and circled over Princess Tamada's grave, chirping day and night. For this reason, Tachibana Toyonichison is said to have built ``Shiratori Shrines'' (Shirataka Shrines) to enshrine swans throughout Japan. The name of Otakayama Shrine is also said to have originated from ``Shiratakasha'' which changed to ``Otakasha''. === Legendary places === The areas along the {{ill|lt=Shiraishi River|Q11580172|WD=Q11580172}}, such as Shibata District and Katta District in the southern part of Miyagi Prefecture, are areas where there is a strong belief in swans, called the ``{{ill|lt=swan|Q34384|WD=Q34384|lt=swan}} faith,'' which reveres swans as messengers of the gods. In addition to Otakayama Shrine, a group of stone monuments known as the ``Shiratori Monument Group'' remain at {{ill|lt=Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine)|Q11396254|WD=Q11396254}}, which is the Engishiki Naimeishin Taisha Shrine of Zao Town Shrine. A similar story related to Takeru Nihon has also survived, and to the south of Kattamine Shrine there are the ``Kosute River'' and ``Kosutegawa Bridge,'' which are said to be the rivers where Nihon Takeru threw the child he left behind by his nurse (the name of the bridge has now been changed to ``Swan Bridge'' because it is associated with child abuse). File:Old stone monuments of Swan at kattamine-jinja shrine.JPG|Swan old monument group
Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine) === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall in the Sankensha irimoya-zukuri style. File:Honden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Main shrine File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 02.JPG|Haden of worship * Iron Nine Ring Tower :: Donated by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Tadahira|Q11623262|WD=Q11623262}}, the third son of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Hidehira|Q3090625|WD=Q3090625}}, during the Bunji period (1185-1189). Currently, only the firebox remains and is incorporated as part of the lantern. * Nanban Iron Torii :: The torii gate is located at the top of the stairs on the approach to the shrine, and is made of steel such as refined cast iron or Western-style cast iron that was brought to Japan from the end of the Muromachi period. It is said to have been dedicated when the company was enshrined in its former shrine at 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district. In addition, there is a mikoshi hall, a bell tower, a shrine office, etc. within the precincts. The precincts are a famous spot for cherry blossom viewing, and are crowded with many worshipers during the cherry blossom season. File:Iron Kurin-tower at Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Iron Kurin-tower File:Ema board of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema in the worship hall
There is a carving that seems to depict the subjugation of Nippon Takeru.File:Nanban-Iron Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Nanban Iron Torii gate File:Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 01.JPG|Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine === Setsusha === * Serikage Shrine :: It is an Ikken-shrine-thatched-style shrine located on the north side of the precincts, and is dedicated to {{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakabu Reimyo}}, {{ill|lt=Hannyas|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyama Hime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyabime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} In the first year ({{ill|lt=1185|Q19729|WD=Q19729}}), {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}{{ill|lt=Tsukuba District|Q7850092|WD=Q7850092}} Kamigun Village It is said to have been enshrined at Kakage Shrine, which is located in ({{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Tsukuba City|Q273863|WD=Q273863}} Kami-gun), and was moved to its current location in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}41. File:Kokage-jinja shrine at Ootakayama-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Kokage Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * {{ill|lt=Waniguchi|Q11674315|WD=Q11674315}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Miyagi Prefecture's designated cultural property: Waniguchi|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/04Kougei/kuni/06waniguti.htm|work= |publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|date= |accessdate=2012-10-20}} :It is said to have been dedicated in the late Kamakura period, during the reign of Emperor Fushimi. There is a long inscription engraved on the body, and the year in the inscription reveals that it was made in 1293. The beginning of the inscription reads, ``Dedication to Otaka Shrine Gohozen Waniguchi Bite,'' and this is the first time that the word ``Waniguchi'' is used in an inscription Explanatory Edition Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 4 Crafts I (Mainichi Shimbunsha, 1981), p. 164. According to the inscription, this crocodile mouth was dedicated to the shrine by ``Hohashi Genno'', who was the official seal of Otakayama Shrine, who took care of it and dedicated it to pray for the long life and longevity of ``Sami Zenmasu,'' a local land leader who was in charge of managing the territory of Otakayama Shrine, and his family. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Okawaramachi|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * “Engishikitaisha Otakayama Shrine” (Otakayama Shrine Office) === External link === * [http://www.ohtakayama.org/ Otakayama Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010200 Otakayama Shrine] (Miyagi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270401.html Otakayama Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otakayama Shinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:大河原町の建築物]] [[Category:大河原町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名おおたかやましんしや]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

link|Q11441957}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Otakayama Shrine |Image = [[File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|270px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Okawaramachi|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|2|18.41|N|140|41|58.55|E|region:JP-04type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otakayama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Yomei|Q349281|WD=Q349281|lt=Tachibana Toyonichi}} |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=572 years|Q49189|WD=Q49189}}?) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |Alternative name = Shiratori Daimyojin, Otakagu Shrine |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|WD=Q2919}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Otakayama Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Otakayama Shrine'' (Otakayama Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Ogawara Town|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nippon Takeru}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Yomei|Q349281|WD=Q349281|lt=Tachibana Toyonichison}}''' ; jointly enshrined deity * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagudo no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyama Mikoto}} * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainatama-mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Fireborn Spirit}} * {{ill|lt=Kikurihime|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Kikurihime}} === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, Otakayama Shrine was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} ({{ill|lt=572|Q49189|WD=Q49189}}) with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nippon Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} as the main deity. According to the shrine's auspicious records, ``Yasunaga Fudoki'' and ``Ou Kanseki Monroshi,'' it is said that Japan Takeru built a temporary shrine and lived there during his eastern expedition to conquer Ezo, and on the site of that temporary shrine, a shrine building called ``Shiratori Daimyojin'' was built and Nihon Takeru was enshrined there. According to tradition, in the second year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suushun|Q349238|WD=Q349238}} ({{ill|lt=588|Q32160|WD=Q32160}}) Tachibana Toyonichison (Emperor Yomei) was enshrined as the main deity. It is said that Emperor Yomei came to this area by imperial order when he was a prince called Tachibana Toyonichison, and that Emperor Yomei's prince, {{ill|lt=Prince Shotoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972}}, enshrined it together at Otakayama Shrine. === Outline === According to "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}", in {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730}}), he was given the divine rank of Jugoinoge (Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade), and {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} In March 11 ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|WD=Q23917}}), he was promoted to Jugoi (Junior Fifth Rank). {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}In March 2020, he was granted the rank of Shoichii, the highest rank in the divine hierarchy, by Sogen Senji. Also, Otakayama Shrine is one of the 100 shrines in Mutsu Province in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, and is ranked among the 15 shrines {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}. In ancient times, it was enshrined on Mt. Daino in Shinkai, Kanegase District (formerly Hira Village, Shibata District) to the northeast of the shrine, but the shrine building was destroyed by a fire in the early {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Genroku period|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine was then relocated to 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district, and in 1992, it was relocated and relocated from Shinkai to its current enshrined location, Kamiyama in the Kanegase district. The torii gate and stone shrine still stand at the old shrine site at Shinkai 126. File:Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Former shrine grounds File:Small shrine of Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrine on the former grounds
The head of the statue and the talisman are enshrined inside. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730}}) May 11th, from no rank to junior fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'') * {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} March 12, 11th year ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|WD=Q23917}}), Upper Fifth Rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') === Swan Faith and Legend === Otakayama Shrine has a legend about Tachibana Toyohichi. === Contents === Tachibana Toyonichi, the prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792}}, set out on a journey touring the eastern regions by imperial order. The residents of Togoku built a palace for Tachibana Toyonichi and warmly welcomed him, and Takashi stayed there for about three years. During that time, he fell in love with Tamayorihime, the daughter of the local ruler, Akasaka Choja. It is said that Tamakihime was a rare beauty with a gentle and chaste personality, and was loved by the noble. One day, Princess Tamaki had a dream in which a swan flew into her body and entered her body, and soon after, a prince was born. Takashi was very pleased and said, ``This is because I worshiped the swan as a god and prayed to it after coming to Mutsu Province.'' After the prince was born, Takashi was ordered to return to the capital, but he was reluctant to say goodbye to Tamakihime, who refused to leave her side, and he promised her that he would send a messenger to meet her in three years. Three years have passed since Takashi thought of Takashi, but the messenger never came and Tamakihime fell ill. The prince's wet nurse could not help but see Tamakihime in such a state, and came to the riverside carrying the prince in her arms. So, she prayed, ``Tamazuhime is thinking about her father, Tachibana Toyonichi, who is about to die of illness.As a prince, you are the embodiment of God, so please take the place of your mother and bring him back to this land,'' and threw the prince into the river. Then, the prince mysteriously turned into a swan and flew from Torigoe village in Fukaya to Yamato, where Takashi lived.Eventually, news of Princess Tamada's death reached Tachibana Toyonichison, and it is said that when he built a magnificent grave for her and buried her, a swan took off into the sky and circled over Princess Tamada's grave, chirping day and night. For this reason, Tachibana Toyonichison is said to have built ``Shiratori Shrines'' (Shirataka Shrines) to enshrine swans throughout Japan. The name of Otakayama Shrine is also said to have originated from ``Shiratakasha'' which changed to ``Otakasha''. === Legendary places === The areas along the {{ill|lt=Shiraishi River|Q11580172|WD=Q11580172}}, such as Shibata District and Katta District in the southern part of Miyagi Prefecture, are areas where there is a strong belief in swans, called the ``{{ill|lt=swan|Q34384|WD=Q34384|lt=swan}} faith,'' which reveres swans as messengers of the gods. In addition to Otakayama Shrine, a group of stone monuments known as the ``Shiratori Monument Group'' remain at {{ill|lt=Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine)|Q11396254|WD=Q11396254}}, which is the Engishiki Naimeishin Taisha Shrine of Zao Town Shrine. A similar story related to Takeru Nihon has also survived, and to the south of Kattamine Shrine there are the ``Kosute River'' and ``Kosutegawa Bridge,'' which are said to be the rivers where Nihon Takeru threw the child he left behind by his nurse (the name of the bridge has now been changed to ``Swan Bridge'' because it is associated with child abuse). File:Old stone monuments of Swan at kattamine-jinja shrine.JPG|Swan old monument group
Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine) === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall in the Sankensha irimoya-zukuri style. File:Honden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Main shrine File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 02.JPG|Haden of worship * Iron Nine Ring Tower :: Donated by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Tadahira|Q11623262|WD=Q11623262}}, the third son of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Hidehira|Q3090625|WD=Q3090625}}, during the Bunji period (1185-1189). Currently, only the firebox remains and is incorporated as part of the lantern. * Nanban Iron Torii :: The torii gate is located at the top of the stairs on the approach to the shrine, and is made of steel such as refined cast iron or Western-style cast iron that was brought to Japan from the end of the Muromachi period. It is said to have been dedicated when the company was enshrined in its former shrine at 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district. In addition, there is a mikoshi hall, a bell tower, a shrine office, etc. within the precincts. The precincts are a famous spot for cherry blossom viewing, and are crowded with many worshipers during the cherry blossom season. File:Iron Kurin-tower at Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Iron Kurin-tower File:Ema board of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema in the worship hall
There is a carving that seems to depict the subjugation of Nippon Takeru.File:Nanban-Iron Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Nanban Iron Torii gate File:Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 01.JPG|Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine === Setsusha === * Serikage Shrine :: It is an Ikken-shrine-thatched-style shrine located on the north side of the precincts, and is dedicated to {{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakabu Reimyo}}, {{ill|lt=Hannyas|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyama Hime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyabime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} In the first year ({{ill|lt=1185|Q19729|WD=Q19729}}), {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}{{ill|lt=Tsukuba District|Q7850092|WD=Q7850092}} Kamigun Village It is said to have been enshrined at Kakage Shrine, which is located in ({{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Tsukuba City|Q273863|WD=Q273863}} Kami-gun), and was moved to its current location in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}41. File:Kokage-jinja shrine at Ootakayama-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Kokage Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * {{ill|lt=Waniguchi|Q11674315|WD=Q11674315}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Miyagi Prefecture's designated cultural property: Waniguchi|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/04Kougei/kuni/06waniguti.htm|work= |publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|date= |accessdate=2012-10-20}} :It is said to have been dedicated in the late Kamakura period, during the reign of Emperor Fushimi. There is a long inscription engraved on the body, and the year in the inscription reveals that it was made in 1293. The beginning of the inscription reads, ``Dedication to Otaka Shrine Gohozen Waniguchi Bite,'' and this is the first time that the word ``Waniguchi'' is used in an inscription Explanatory Edition Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 4 Crafts I (Mainichi Shimbunsha, 1981), p. 164. According to the inscription, this crocodile mouth was dedicated to the shrine by ``Hohashi Genno'', who was the official seal of Otakayama Shrine, who took care of it and dedicated it to pray for the long life and longevity of ``Sami Zenmasu,'' a local land leader who was in charge of managing the territory of Otakayama Shrine, and his family. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Okawaramachi|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * “Engishikitaisha Otakayama Shrine” (Otakayama Shrine Office) === External link === * [http://www.ohtakayama.org/ Otakayama Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010200 Otakayama Shrine] (Miyagi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270401.html Otakayama Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otakayama Shinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:大河原町の建築物]] [[Category:大河原町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名おおたかやましんしや]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Okawaramachi|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|38|2|18.41|N|140|41|58.55|E|region:JP-04type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Otakayama Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nihon Takeru}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Yomei|Q349281|WD=Q349281|lt=Tachibana Toyonichi}} |Company status = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Meishin University}})
Old {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |Foundation = (tradition) {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=572 years|Q49189|WD=Q49189}}?) |Main shrine = Sangensha {{ill|lt=Irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} |Alternative name = Shiratori Daimyojin, Otakagu Shrine |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 15th|Q2919|WD=Q2919}}|Map = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Otakayama Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Otakayama Shrine'' (Otakayama Shrine) is located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Ogawara Town|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}. It is {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined God === ; Main enshrined deity * '''{{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Nippon Takeru}}''' * '''{{ill|lt=Emperor Yomei|Q349281|WD=Q349281|lt=Tachibana Toyonichison}}''' ; jointly enshrined deity * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Kagudo no Mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Ooyamatsumi|Q386563|WD=Q386563|lt=Oyama Mikoto}} * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Kurainatama-mikoto}} * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Fireborn Spirit}} * {{ill|lt=Kikurihime|UNKNOWN|WD=Q11619891|lt=Hakusan Kikurihime}} === History === === Founding === According to the shrine's legend, Otakayama Shrine was founded in the first year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|Q316637|WD=Q316637}} ({{ill|lt=572|Q49189|WD=Q49189}}) with {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nippon Takeru|1=UNKNOWN}} as the main deity. According to the shrine's auspicious records, ``Yasunaga Fudoki'' and ``Ou Kanseki Monroshi,'' it is said that Japan Takeru built a temporary shrine and lived there during his eastern expedition to conquer Ezo, and on the site of that temporary shrine, a shrine building called ``Shiratori Daimyojin'' was built and Nihon Takeru was enshrined there. According to tradition, in the second year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suushun|Q349238|WD=Q349238}} ({{ill|lt=588|Q32160|WD=Q32160}}) Tachibana Toyonichison (Emperor Yomei) was enshrined as the main deity. It is said that Emperor Yomei came to this area by imperial order when he was a prince called Tachibana Toyonichison, and that Emperor Yomei's prince, {{ill|lt=Prince Shotoku|Q263972|WD=Q263972}}, enshrined it together at Otakayama Shrine. === Outline === According to "{{ill|lt=Shoku Nihon Koki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}", in {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730}}), he was given the divine rank of Jugoinoge (Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade), and {{ill|lt=Jogan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} In March 11 ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|WD=Q23917}}), he was promoted to Jugoi (Junior Fifth Rank). {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}In March 2020, he was granted the rank of Shoichii, the highest rank in the divine hierarchy, by Sogen Senji. Also, Otakayama Shrine is one of the 100 shrines in Mutsu Province in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmecho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}, and is ranked among the 15 shrines {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}. In ancient times, it was enshrined on Mt. Daino in Shinkai, Kanegase District (formerly Hira Village, Shibata District) to the northeast of the shrine, but the shrine building was destroyed by a fire in the early {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Genroku period|1=UNKNOWN}}. The shrine was then relocated to 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district, and in 1992, it was relocated and relocated from Shinkai to its current enshrined location, Kamiyama in the Kanegase district. The torii gate and stone shrine still stand at the old shrine site at Shinkai 126. File:Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 1.JPG|Former shrine grounds File:Small shrine of Former shrine of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrine on the former grounds
The head of the statue and the talisman are enshrined inside. === God level === * {{ill|lt=Jowa (Japan)|Q1075605|WD=Q1075605|lt=Jowa}} 9th year ({{ill|lt=842|Q24730|WD=Q24730}}) May 11th, from no rank to junior fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Shoku Nippon Ki|Q1041301|WD=Q1041301}}'') * {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōkan}} March 12, 11th year ({{ill|lt=869|Q23917|WD=Q23917}}), Upper Fifth Rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') === Swan Faith and Legend === Otakayama Shrine has a legend about Tachibana Toyohichi. === Contents === Tachibana Toyonichi, the prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kinmei|Q329792|WD=Q329792}}, set out on a journey touring the eastern regions by imperial order. The residents of Togoku built a palace for Tachibana Toyonichi and warmly welcomed him, and Takashi stayed there for about three years. During that time, he fell in love with Tamayorihime, the daughter of the local ruler, Akasaka Choja. It is said that Tamakihime was a rare beauty with a gentle and chaste personality, and was loved by the noble. One day, Princess Tamaki had a dream in which a swan flew into her body and entered her body, and soon after, a prince was born. Takashi was very pleased and said, ``This is because I worshiped the swan as a god and prayed to it after coming to Mutsu Province.'' After the prince was born, Takashi was ordered to return to the capital, but he was reluctant to say goodbye to Tamakihime, who refused to leave her side, and he promised her that he would send a messenger to meet her in three years. Three years have passed since Takashi thought of Takashi, but the messenger never came and Tamakihime fell ill. The prince's wet nurse could not help but see Tamakihime in such a state, and came to the riverside carrying the prince in her arms. So, she prayed, ``Tamazuhime is thinking about her father, Tachibana Toyonichi, who is about to die of illness.As a prince, you are the embodiment of God, so please take the place of your mother and bring him back to this land,'' and threw the prince into the river. Then, the prince mysteriously turned into a swan and flew from Torigoe village in Fukaya to Yamato, where Takashi lived.Eventually, news of Princess Tamada's death reached Tachibana Toyonichison, and it is said that when he built a magnificent grave for her and buried her, a swan took off into the sky and circled over Princess Tamada's grave, chirping day and night. For this reason, Tachibana Toyonichison is said to have built ``Shiratori Shrines'' (Shirataka Shrines) to enshrine swans throughout Japan. The name of Otakayama Shrine is also said to have originated from ``Shiratakasha'' which changed to ``Otakasha''. === Legendary places === The areas along the {{ill|lt=Shiraishi River|Q11580172|WD=Q11580172}}, such as Shibata District and Katta District in the southern part of Miyagi Prefecture, are areas where there is a strong belief in swans, called the ``{{ill|lt=swan|Q34384|WD=Q34384|lt=swan}} faith,'' which reveres swans as messengers of the gods. In addition to Otakayama Shrine, a group of stone monuments known as the ``Shiratori Monument Group'' remain at {{ill|lt=Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine)|Q11396254|WD=Q11396254}}, which is the Engishiki Naimeishin Taisha Shrine of Zao Town Shrine. A similar story related to Takeru Nihon has also survived, and to the south of Kattamine Shrine there are the ``Kosute River'' and ``Kosutegawa Bridge,'' which are said to be the rivers where Nihon Takeru threw the child he left behind by his nurse (the name of the bridge has now been changed to ``Swan Bridge'' because it is associated with child abuse). File:Old stone monuments of Swan at kattamine-jinja shrine.JPG|Swan old monument group
Kattamine Shrine (Zao Town Shrine) === Precincts === The shrine building consists of the worship hall, the shrine hall, and the main hall in the Sankensha irimoya-zukuri style. File:Honden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Main shrine File:Haiden of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 02.JPG|Haden of worship * Iron Nine Ring Tower :: Donated by {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Tadahira|Q11623262|WD=Q11623262}}, the third son of {{ill|lt=Fujiwara Hidehira|Q3090625|WD=Q3090625}}, during the Bunji period (1185-1189). Currently, only the firebox remains and is incorporated as part of the lantern. * Nanban Iron Torii :: The torii gate is located at the top of the stairs on the approach to the shrine, and is made of steel such as refined cast iron or Western-style cast iron that was brought to Japan from the end of the Muromachi period. It is said to have been dedicated when the company was enshrined in its former shrine at 126 Shinkai in the Kanegase district. In addition, there is a mikoshi hall, a bell tower, a shrine office, etc. within the precincts. The precincts are a famous spot for cherry blossom viewing, and are crowded with many worshipers during the cherry blossom season. File:Iron Kurin-tower at Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Iron Kurin-tower File:Ema board of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Ema in the worship hall
There is a carving that seems to depict the subjugation of Nippon Takeru.File:Nanban-Iron Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine.JPG|Nanban Iron Torii gate File:Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine 01.JPG|Torii gate of Ootakayama-jinja shrine === Setsusha === * Serikage Shrine :: It is an Ikken-shrine-thatched-style shrine located on the north side of the precincts, and is dedicated to {{ill|WD=Q10921330|lt=Wakumusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Wakabu Reimyo}}, {{ill|lt=Hannyas|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyama Hime no Mikoto}}, and {{ill|lt=Konohanasakuyabime|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. {{ill|lt=Bunji|Q1194415|WD=Q1194415}} In the first year ({{ill|lt=1185|Q19729|WD=Q19729}}), {{ill|lt=Hitachikuni|Q1055353|WD=Q1055353}}{{ill|lt=Tsukuba District|Q7850092|WD=Q7850092}} Kamigun Village It is said to have been enshrined at Kakage Shrine, which is located in ({{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|WD=Q83273}}{{ill|lt=Tsukuba City|Q273863|WD=Q273863}} Kami-gun), and was moved to its current location in {{ill|lt=Showa|Q327567|WD=Q327567}}41. File:Kokage-jinja shrine at Ootakayama-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Kokage Shrine === Cultural assets === === Important cultural property (designated by the country) === * {{ill|lt=Waniguchi|Q11674315|WD=Q11674315}}{{Cite web|Japanese book|title=Miyagi Prefecture's designated cultural property: Waniguchi|url=http://www.pref.miyagi.jp/bunkazai/siteibunkazai/miyagi-no-bunkazai/04Kougei/kuni/06waniguti.htm|work= |publisher=Miyagi Prefecture|date= |accessdate=2012-10-20}} :It is said to have been dedicated in the late Kamakura period, during the reign of Emperor Fushimi. There is a long inscription engraved on the body, and the year in the inscription reveals that it was made in 1293. The beginning of the inscription reads, ``Dedication to Otaka Shrine Gohozen Waniguchi Bite,'' and this is the first time that the word ``Waniguchi'' is used in an inscription Explanatory Edition Newly Designated Important Cultural Properties 4 Crafts I (Mainichi Shimbunsha, 1981), p. 164. According to the inscription, this crocodile mouth was dedicated to the shrine by ``Hohashi Genno'', who was the official seal of Otakayama Shrine, who took care of it and dedicated it to pray for the long life and longevity of ``Sami Zenmasu,'' a local land leader who was in charge of managing the territory of Otakayama Shrine, and his family. === Local information === ; Location * {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Shibata District|Q1131788|WD=Q1131788}}{{ill|lt=Okawaramachi|Q1155682|WD=Q1155682}}Kanagase Kamiyama 45 === Source === {{Reflist}} === References === * “Engishikitaisha Otakayama Shrine” (Otakayama Shrine Office) === External link === * [http://www.ohtakayama.org/ Otakayama Shrine] (official website) * [http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010200 Otakayama Shrine] (Miyagi Prefectural Shrine Agency) * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/270401.html Otakayama Shrine] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program “Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection”) {{Shinto horizontal}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otakayama Shinsiya}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōtakayama Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:大河原町の建築物]] [[Category:大河原町の歴史]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社|名おおたかやましんしや]] [[Category:宮城県の旧郷社]] [[Category:宮城県の重要文化財]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]
